title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-10 · Halalan sa Senado ng Maine: Platner vs. Collins
summary: Sa Democratic primary ng Maine, si Graham Platter, na nababalot ng iskandalo, ay nanalo laban kay Gobernador Janet Mills na may humigit-kumulang 75% ng boto, at haharapin ang Republican Senator Susan Collins. Kailangan ni Platter na makuha ang suporta ng mga tagasuporta ni Mills, samantalang si Collins ay may kalamangan pa rin sa mga independenteng botante. Iba pang balita: Naglunsad ang US ng missile strike sa Iran; Nag-apply ang OpenAI para sa IPO; Pagdinig sa Epstein files; Kontrobersyal na pahayag ni Victor Marx, kandidato para sa gobernador ng Colorado.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today in the group chat, Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner. Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall? REP. ALEXANDRIA OCASIO-CORTEZ (D-NY): If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon sa group chat, binoto ng mga Democrat ng Maine si Graham Platter. Mawawalan ba sila ng pagkakataong makuha ang Senado ngayong taglagas? Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-NY): Kung ang pagpipilian sa balota ay iyon at isang senador na bumoto para alisin ang health insurance ng milyun-milyong Amerikano, iyon ang sitwasyong kailangan nating timbangin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again. Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long- promised deal with Iran? And a big day for the Epstein files. What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
paragraphs.cn: Muling nagliwanag ang mga missile sa kalangitan ng Gitnang Silangan. Magtatagal ba ang marupok na tigil-putukan upang matupad ng US ang pangako nitong pangmatagalang kasunduan sa Iran? Mahalagang araw para sa mga Epstein files. Ano ang sasabihin ni Bill Gates sa mga mambabatas tungkol sa kanyang kaugnayan sa yumaong convicted sex offender?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: Sa tingin mo ba ay nakapatay ka ng tao noong matanda ka na? Victor Marx (R),

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child. Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado. And it's the $3.5 trillion question on Wall Street. Has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself?
paragraphs.cn: Ito ay isang kandidato na nagsabing pumatay siya noong bata pa siya. Kilalanin ang nangunguna sa Republican primary para sa gobernador ng Colorado. Ito ang $3.5 trilyong tanong ng Wall Street. Nauuna na ba ang AI boom?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: Plano ng OpenAI at Anthropic na magsagawa ng initial public offering sa katapusan ng taong ito, na nagpapahiwatig sa akin na malapit nang sumabog ang AI bubble. Graham Platter (D),

paragraphs.speaker: Maine Senate Candidate
paragraphs.en: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support. And I will spend every day of this campaign. And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
paragraphs.cn: Para sa sinumang nadidismaya o nabigo, ang trabaho ko ay makuha ang inyong tiwala, paniniwala, at suporta. Gagawin ko ito araw-araw sa kampanyang ito. At kung magkakaroon ako ng pagkakataong mapabilang sa Senado ng US, gagawin ko ito araw-araw.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Graham Platner punches his ticket. But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator. Good morning everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Here is where we begin. That big statement out of Maine. Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three- fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills. Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Nanalo si Graham Platter sa nominasyon. Ngunit kailangan niyang kumbinsihin ang mga botante na piliin siya sa halip na isang senador na limang beses nang nahalal. Magandang umaga sa inyong lahat. Ako si Audie Cornish. Simulan na natin. Malaking balita mula sa Maine. Si Graham Platter, Democrat, sa kabila ng mga iskandalo, ay nanalo ng humigit-kumulang tatlong-kuwarto ng boto sa primary, tinalo si Gobernador Janet Mills. Di-nagtagal matapos makuha ang nominasyon, inilipat niya ang kanyang atensyon sa kanyang bagong kalaban, Republican Senator Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Platner
paragraphs.en: Susan Collins may have started -- may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves. If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
paragraphs.cn: Maaaring may mabuting intensyon si Susan Collins noong sinimulan niya ang kanyang karera sa Washington ilang dekada na ang nakalipas, ngunit siya ay naging walang gulugod at corrupt tulad ng establishment na kanyang pinaglilingkuran. Kung ikaw ay isang independenteng boses, bakit 95% ng oras ay bumoto ka para kay Donald Trump?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
paragraphs.cn: Kailangan ni Platter ng maraming trabaho upang mapunta sa kanyang panig ang mga tagasuporta ni Mills.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
paragraphs.cn: Hindi ko talaga kayang suportahan si Platter, kahit na maganda ang ilan sa kanyang sinasabi.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I don't think he's ready to be a senator. I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet. And --
paragraphs.cn: Sa tingin ko hindi pa siya handa maging senador. Mas gusto kong suportahan si Collins, maliban kung magpakita ng iba ang taong iyon, pero hindi pa siya -- hindi pa siya ganun. At --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, kung si Platner laban kay Collins sa Nobyembre, ano ang gagawin mo?

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: That's a really good question. I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine. But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
paragraphs.cn: Magandang tanong iyan. Ako ay isang Democrat, ngunit maraming beses akong bumoto para kay Susan Collins dahil naniniwala akong talagang nagmamalasakit siya sa Maine. Ngunit ang Nobyembre ay magiging isang tunay na problema para sa akin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, today in the group chat, Mike Leon, host of the "Can We Please Talk" podcast; Jared Stepman, columnist with "The Daily Signal"; and Max Rose, former Democratic congressman from New York. And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race. You guys have been giving a lot of money across the country. And what is it? Let me just show what -- what's at stake here. Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, ngayon sa group chat, narito ang host ng podcast na 'Can We Talk' na si Mike Leon, ang columnist ng 'Daily Signal' na si Jared Steppman, at dating New York Democratic Congressman na si Max Rose. Max, gusto ko munang tanungin ka, dahil nagtatrabaho ka sa VoteVets, ibig sabihin may interes din kayo sa karerang ito. Malaking pera ang inilagay ninyo sa buong bansa. Kaya, ano ang sitwasyon? Hayaan mong ipakita ko -- -- narito ang susi. Ang pagkakataon ng Democrats na makuha ang upuan ng Senado ng Maine.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It was pretty good. Maybe this has jeopardized it. We're going to show people a chart while you're talking so you can see what's at stake. Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals? MAX ROSE (D),
paragraphs.cn: Medyo maganda. Baka nanganganib na ito. Magpapakita kami ng tsart sa mga tao habang nagsasalita ka para makita nila ang nakataya. Dahil nahihirapan si Graham sa mga iskandalo, gaano kalapit ang karerang ito tulad ng inaasahan ninyong lahat? Max Rose (Democrat):

paragraphs.speaker: Former New York Congressman
paragraphs.en: The race is always going to be close. Because --
paragraphs.cn: Ang karerang ito ay palaging magiging malapit. Dahil -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Not with a five-term senator, it's not. A lot of times it can be a blowout, right? Like what -- how do you look at what he needs to accomplish?
paragraphs.cn: Para sa isang limang-term na senador, hindi ganoon. Madalas ito ay maaaring maging isang tabi, tama? Tulad ng -- -- paano mo nakikita ang kailangan niyang gawin?

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Well, I think that we're saying the same thing --
paragraphs.cn: Well, sa tingin ko pareho ang sinasabi natin -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: -- in essence, right? Susan Collins is tough to beat. What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race. You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- sa esensya, tama? Mahirap talunin si Susan Collins. Ang gusto kong sabihin tungkol sa kampanya ni Susan Collins ay, sa nakaraan nakita natin na hindi mo maaaring manalo sa upuan na ito sa pamamagitan ng pagpili ng 'light version ni Susan Collins' o paglalagay ng 'light version ni Susan Collins' sa karera. Tatalunin mo siya sa pamamagitan ng pag-udyok sa iyong base, lalo na sa midterm elections.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: There is no doubt, though, that this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins: on the ways in which she has voted to empower Donald Trump; on her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable; on the ways in which she has said, I might be fair, but I want to take healthcare away from millions of people. Says the right thing often; does the wrong thing very frequently.
paragraphs.cn: Gayunpaman, walang duda na ang halalan na ito ay dapat na isang referendum kay Susan Collins: tungkol sa paraan ng kanyang pagboto na nagbigay kapangyarihan kay Donald Trump; tungkol sa paraan ng kanyang pagtanggi na panagutin si Donald Trump; tungkol sa paraan ng kanyang sinabi, maaaring patas ako, ngunit gusto kong alisin ang healthcare mula sa milyun-milyong tao. Madalas siyang nagsasabi ng tamang bagay; ngunit napakadalas na gumagawa ng maling bagay.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. So, this is very much the message we hear over and over again. And I love that Platner in front of him, it said, "They don't know Maine." You know what I mean? Usually not his own name, not a bunch of other things. It was like, hello, media, this one is for you. I want to play two clips back-to-back, because it sort of, I think, represents something of this race. First, I'm going to play for you J.D. Vance and how he talks about Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Kaya, iyon mismo ang paulit-ulit naming naririnig na mensahe. Gusto ko ang karatula ni Platner sa harap niya na nagsasabing 'Hindi nila kilala ang Maine'. Naiintindihan mo ba ang ibig kong sabihin? Karaniwan hindi ang kanyang pangalan, o iba pang mga bagay. Para bang sinasabi, media, ito ay para sa inyo. Gusto kong magpatugtog ng dalawang audio clip nang sunud-sunod, dahil sa tingin ko ito ay kumakatawan sa ilang katangian ng karerang ito. Una, ipapatugtog ko ang clip ni J.D. Vance na nagsasalita tungkol kay Susan Collins.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And then I'm going to play AOC and how she's talking about Platner. You tell me how you hear the tone of these. J.D. VANCE (R),
paragraphs.cn: Pagkatapos ay ipapatugtog ko ang clip ni AOC na nagsasalita tungkol kay Platner. Sabihin mo sa akin kung paano mo nararamdaman ang mga tono na ito. J.D. Vance (Republican):

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. Vice President
paragraphs.en: Here's the thing I'll say about Susan Collins, is sometimes I get frustrated with Susan Collins. I almost wish that she was more partisan. But the thing I love about Susan is she is independent, because Maine is an independent state. And frankly, if she was as partisan as I sometimes wish that she was, she would not be a good fit for the people of Maine. So, let's give a shout-out to Susan Collins, who's doing a great job.
paragraphs.cn: Tungkol kay Susan Collins, ang sasabihin ko ay, minsan nabibigo ako sa kanya. Halos gusto ko sana siyang maging mas partisan. Ngunit ang gusto ko kay Susan ay, siya ay independyente, dahil ang Maine ay isang independiyenteng estado. Sa totoo lang, kung siya ay kasing partisan gaya ng gusto ko minsan, hindi siya bagay sa mga tao ng Maine. Kaya, palakpakan natin si Susan Collins, mahusay ang kanyang ginagawa.

paragraphs.speaker: Ocasio-Cortez
paragraphs.en: When it comes to the substance of this reporting, obviously, there's a lot in that behavior that's really challenging. It's hard to stomach, you know, in some of it. But -- but at the end of the day, I think that this is a choice. If that choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take healthcare away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: Sa nilalaman ng ulat na ito, malinaw na maraming nakakabahala sa mga kilos na iyon. May mga bahaging mahirap tanggapin. Ngunit sa huli, sa tingin ko ito ay isang pagpili. Kung ang pagpipilian sa balota ay sa pagitan niyon at isang senador na nag-aalis ng segurong pangkalusugan ng milyun-milyong Amerikano, iyon ang sitwasyong kailangan nating timbangin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I played these, because one is like, it's hard to stomach. And the other is like, I get frustrated. Shout-out though, anyway, because I guess Maine. And I was, like, these are not ringing endorsements. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?"
paragraphs.cn: Pinatugtog ko ang mga ito dahil ang isa ay mahirap tanggapin, at ang isa naman ay nakakadismaya. Pero palakpakan pa rin, dahil sa tingin ko ay Maine. Naisip ko, hindi ito masigasig na suporta. Mike Leon, host, "Maaari ba nating pag-usapan ito?"

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.en: But Audie, not only are they not ringing endorsements, the voter that we played in the package before listen to her tone. Maine independents are the second largest party in Maine.
paragraphs.cn: Pero Audi, hindi lang ito hindi masigasig na suporta. Ang botante na pinatugtog natin sa naunang bahagi, pakinggan ang tono niya. Ang mga independiyente sa Maine ang pangalawang pinakamalaking partido sa estado.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And a woman, in particular.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, lalo na ang mga kababaihan.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: And a woman and a Democrat who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will, it sounded like, from that thing. So -- but those are -- those are some of the things that we're going to have to consider now. It's really about the character quality is what something that Graham Platner has been trying to say: Forget about that. I'm from here. It's how she's voted, to Max's point.
paragraphs.cn: Isang babaeng Democrat, sinabi niya na bumoto siya para kay Susan Collins, pero parang labag sa kanyang kalooban. Kaya -- -- pero ito ang mga bagay na kailangan nating isaalang-alang ngayon. Ito ay tungkol sa mga katangian ng karakter, na siyang sinasabi ni Graham Plattner: kalimutan ang mga iyon. Galing ako rito. Ang mahalaga ay ang kanyang talaan ng pagboto, gaya ng sinabi ni Max.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: But then also you hear the voters, and they're, like, a little, still unsure because of those character issues with Platner. So, it's going to be really interesting.
paragraphs.cn: Pero naririnig mo rin ang mga botante, medyo hindi pa sila sigurado dahil sa mga isyu sa karakter ni Plattner. Kaya, ito ay magiging napaka-interesante.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And he's been telling a story for a while of, like, you know, I was -- went through difficult times. Now I'm a person who has changed. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond. OK? Can I just put it that way? Here's a list I'm going to play for you. Just some moments over the past couple of months where you hear her talking around Trump policies and where she stands on them. SEN. SUSAN COLLINS (R-ME): The 60 days is a trigger.
paragraphs.cn: At siya ay nagkukuwento, tulad ng, alam mo, dumaan ako sa mahihirap na panahon. Ngayon ako ay isang nagbagong tao. Gusto kong magtanong ng isa pang bagay. Si Collins at Trump, hindi sila magkalapit. Okay? Masasabi ko ba iyon? Narito ang isang listahan, ipapatugtog ko ito para sa iyo. Ilang sandali lang mula sa nakaraang ilang buwan, naririnig mo siyang pinag-uusapan ang mga patakaran ni Trump at ang kanyang paninindigan sa mga ito. Senador Susan Collins (R-Maine): Ang 60 araw ay isang trigger point.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: After 60 days, in my view, the president has to obtain congressional approval or Congress can block it. I do not believe that individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers on January 6th should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees. Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid and the impact on my state, but other states, as well. I've talked with the secretary of homeland security.
paragraphs.cn: Pagkatapos ng 60 araw, sa aking pananaw, ang pangulo ay dapat kumuha ng pag-apruba ng Kongreso, kung hindi ay maaaring hadlangan ito ng Kongreso. Hindi ako naniniwala na ang mga nahatulan ng marahas na pag-atake sa pulisya noong Enero 6 ay dapat maging karapat-dapat sa reimbursement ng legal na gastos. Nag-aalala ako sa epekto ng pagbawas ng Medicaid sa aking estado at sa iba pang estado. Nakausap ko na ang Kalihim ng Homeland Security.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I have strongly recommended to her that there be a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minnesota.
paragraphs.cn: Mahigpit kong iminumungkahi na suspindihin niya ang mga operasyon ng pagpapatupad sa Maine at Minnesota.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, I've chased her in the hallways before, too. She often ends up voting with Republicans, whatever that party line is. But is Trump going to get involved in this simply because of what's at stake with the Senate? Or is he going to send surrogates? He'll be like, OK, J.D., you go there? Like, can she expect any support there? JARRETT STEPMAN, COLUMNIST, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes, I feel that's why he sent J.D. and I feel he will, if he's smart, actually keep some distance from this.
paragraphs.cn: Dati ko na rin siyang hinabol sa hallway. Madalas siyang bumoto kasama ng mga Republican, anuman ang linya ng partido. Pero makikialam ba si Trump dahil lamang sa nakataya sa Senado? O magpapadala siya ng mga kinatawan? Sasabihin ba niya, okay, J.D., pumunta ka doon? Maaasahan ba niya ang suporta? Columnist ng Daily Signal na si Jarrett Stepman: Oo, sa tingin ko iyon ang dahilan kung bakit pinapunta niya si J.D., at sa tingin ko kung siya ay matalino, lalayo siya rito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I mean part of the -- the thing at stake here is that they're independent. Maine is a little bit different. I mean, both these candidates come from sort of -- almost independent of the party.
paragraphs.cn: Ang ibig kong sabihin, bahagi ng nakataya ay sila ay independiyente. Medyo naiiba ang Maine. Ang ibig kong sabihin, ang dalawang kandidatong ito ay parehong nagmula sa isang uri ng -- -- halos independiyente sa partido.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: Because they -- they really focus on the issues related to Maine.
paragraphs.cn: Dahil talagang nakatuon sila sa mga isyung may kaugnayan sa Maine.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We should say, Platner is recruiting from outside the party. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Dapat nating sabihin, si Plattner ay kumukuha ng mga tao mula sa labas ng partido. Oo.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: Exactly. So, they're both sort of weirdly outsiders, even though Collins has been Republican part of the party for a long time, she's always struck an independent game this entire time. So, I think that's going to continue during this race. That is her path. Because if she does get too close to Trump, that's going to be the argument saying, whoa, she's too MAGA. She's not -- too much for me, which is more or less a blue state.
paragraphs.cn: Tama. Kaya, pareho silang medyo kakaiba na mga tagalabas, kahit na si Collins ay matagal nang bahagi ng Republican Party, pinanatili niya ang isang malayang tindig. Kaya, sa tingin ko ang sitwasyong ito ay magpapatuloy sa kampanyang ito. Ito ang kanyang daan. Dahil kung lalapit siya kay Trump, may magsasabi, wow, sobrang MAGA niya. Hindi siya para sa akin, at ito ay isang blue state.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you're saying there's nothing to be gained there of him coming.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, sinasabi mo na wala siyang mapapala sa pagpunta.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, you guys stay with me, because we're going to talk about a couple other states this hour, as well. We're also still waiting for news about some sort of proposed deal with Iran. But in the meantime, the U.S. has launched new strikes against Iran overnight. Plus, Epstein's former assistant testifies before Congress. What she says she did not see. And a Texas teenager convicted of killing a rival track star. Why the jury rejected his claim of self-defense.
paragraphs.cn: Sige, manatili kayo sa amin dahil sa oras na ito tatalakayin din natin ang ilang iba pang estado. Naghihintay din tayo ng balita tungkol sa isang iminungkahing kasunduan sa Iran. Ngunit samantala, ang US ay nagsagawa ng mga bagong welga laban sa Iran sa magdamag. Dagdag pa rito, ang dating assistant ni Epstein ay nagpatotoo sa Kongreso. Sinabi niya na wala siyang nakita. At isang teenager mula Texas ay hinatulan ng pagpatay sa isang karibal na track star. Bakit tinanggihan ng hurado ang kanyang depensa ng self-defense.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: It's just sad that it had to come to this. It was just a bad, bad decision he made.
paragraphs.cn: Nakalulungkot na umabot ito sa puntong ito. Gumawa siya ng isang napakasamang desisyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right now, we are following the latest round of escalation between the U.S. and Iran. U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND saying they are now finished hitting targets in Iran in response to an Army helicopter getting shot down over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's state media, however, released new video overnight, saying they retaliated to the U.S. by attack -- by launching missiles against targets in Bahrain and Kuwait and an air base in Jordan.
paragraphs.cn: Sa kasalukuyan, sinusubaybayan natin ang pinakabagong round ng tensyon sa pagitan ng US at Iran. Sinabi ng US Central Command na natapos na nila ang mga welga sa mga target sa Iran bilang tugon sa pagbagsak ng isang Army helicopter sa Strait of Hormuz. Gayunpaman, ang Iranian state media ay naglabas ng bagong video sa magdamag, na nagsasabing naghiganti sila sa US sa pamamagitan ng pagpapaputok ng mga missile sa mga target sa Bahrain, Kuwait, at isang air base sa Jordan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining me now with the latest, CNN international correspondent Paula Hancocks in Abu Dhabi. Paula, can you talk about this? We know Iran, throughout the last couple of weeks, has -- they've tried to spread the war throughout the region. What have you heard from officials in Kuwait or Bahrain about confirmed damage?
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon ay kumonekta tayo kay Paula Hancocks, CNN International correspondent, na nasa Abu Dhabi para sa pinakabagong mga ulat. Paula, maaari mo bang talakayin ang sitwasyong ito? Alam natin na sinubukan ng Iran na palawakin ang digmaan sa buong rehiyon sa nakalipas na ilang linggo. Nakarinig ka ba mula sa mga opisyal ng Kuwait o Bahrain tungkol sa pagkumpirma ng pinsala?

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: The next few hours are going to be crucial as to whether or not this is an isolated escalation between the U.S. and Iran, or whether it is a step on the escalatory ladder. Now, at this point, we have not heard any damage assessment or casualty figures from those countries which were targeted by Iran. That is, Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain. Iran said that it had targeted the fifth fleet headquarters in Bahrain.
paragraphs.cn: Ang mga susunod na oras ay magiging kritikal, na magpapasiya kung ito ay isang isolated escalation sa pagitan ng US at Iran o isang hakbang sa escalation ladder. Sa ngayon, wala pa tayong naririnig na anumang pagtatasa ng pinsala o bilang ng nasawi mula sa mga bansang target ng Iran -- Jordan, Kuwait, at Bahrain. Sinabi ng Iran na sinalakay nila ang headquarters ng Fifth Fleet sa Bahrain.

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: They also say that they targeted a military base in Jordan, Kuwait, as well, saying that it was intercepting incoming aerial missiles and drones. So, what we've heard from Iran's side is that they have said that there will -- no attack will go unanswered from the United States. What we had seen from U.S.
paragraphs.cn: Inaangkin din nila na sinalakay nila ang isang military base sa Jordan at Kuwait, at sinabi na ang base ay humaharang sa mga paparating na aerial missiles at drones. Ang naririnig natin mula sa panig ng Iran ay sinabi nila na walang pag-atake mula sa US ang hindi sasagutin. Ang nakikita natin mula sa panig ng US ay...

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: CENTRAL COMMAND is they said they had carried out what they called a, quote, "proportional response" to the shooting down of that Apache helicopter, saying that there were three locations along the Strait of Hormuz that were targeted. They say that they hit air defense, ground control stations, radar sites, as well. Now, one U.S.
paragraphs.cn: Sinabi ng US Central Command na nagsagawa sila ng tinatawag na 'proportional response' sa pagbagsak ng Apache helicopter, na sinasabing tinamaan ang tatlong lokasyon sa baybayin ng Strait of Hormuz. Sinabi nila na tinamaan din nila ang air defense system, ground control station, at radar station. Ngayon, sinabi ng isang US official sa CNN na ito ay talagang para magpadala ng babala sa Iran, umaasa sila na manatili sa track ang negosasyon at hindi hadlangan ang mga ito, kahit na narinig natin mula sa tagapagsalita ng Iranian Foreign Ministry na muling sinusuri ng Iran ang negosasyon, sinisisi ang US sa kakulangan ng sincerity sa negosasyon, Ody.

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.en: official telling CNN that this was really intended to be a warning to Iran, and they do hope that they will still be able to keep the talks on track, that it won't hinder those talks, although we've heard from Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson that Iran is reviewing negotiations at this point, accusing the U.S. of negotiating in bad faith, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: Sige. Iyan ang pinakabagong ulat mula kay Paula Hancocks. Maraming salamat. 17 minuto na lampas ng oras. Gusto kong ibigay sa iyo ang morning briefing. Ngayon, ang House Oversight Committee ay makikinig sa testimonya ni Bill Gates, ang bilyonaryo ng Microsoft, sa closed session. Plano niyang tumestigo tungkol sa kanyang propesyonal na relasyon kay Jeffrey Epstein. Si Gates ay hindi inaakusahan ng anumang maling gawain.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. That's Paula Hancocks with that update. Thank you so much. It is now 17 minutes past the hour. I want to give you your morning roundup. Happening today, the House Oversight Committee will hear from Microsoft billionaire Bill Gates behind closed doors. He's scheduled to testify about his professional relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Gates has not been accused of wrongdoing.
paragraphs.cn: Samantala, ang dating assistant ni Epstein na si Lesley Groff ay nagpatotoo noong Martes na sinabi niyang 'hindi siya kailanman nakasaksi ng anumang hindi nararapat o ilegal na pag-uugali.' Sinabi rin niya sa mga mambabatas na inayos niya ang maraming tawag sa telepono sa pagitan ni Epstein at Donald Trump bago naging presidente si Trump. Si Pangulong Trump ay hindi inaakusahan ng anumang maling gawain kaugnay kay Epstein. Sa Texas, isang teenager ang hinatulan ng murder dahil sa pagsaksak ng 17-taong-gulang na track star mula sa kalabang koponan, at sinentensiyahan ng 35 taon sa bilangguan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, Epstein's former assistant, Lesley Groff, testified on Tuesday, saying that she, quote, "never witnessed anything improper or illegal." She also told lawmakers she arranged multiple calls between Epstein and Donald Trump before he was president. President Trump is not accused of any wrongdoing in connection to Epstein. And in Texas, a teen who fatally stabbed a 17-year-old track athlete from a rival team has been convicted of murder and sentenced to 35 years in prison.
paragraphs.cn: Tinanggihan ng hurado ang pag-aangkin ni Carmelo Anthony na siya ay nag-self-defense noong nakaraang taon sa isang sagupaan kay Austin Metcalfe sa stadium bleachers.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: A jury rejected Karmelo Anthony's claims of self-defense during a confrontation with Austin Metcalf in the stadium bleachers last year.
paragraphs.cn: Nakakalungkot na umabot sa ganito. Gumawa siya ng napakasamang desisyon. Pero kumuha siya ng buhay. Sa kasamaang palad, dalawang batang buhay ang naapektuhan nang napaka-negatibo.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: It's just sad that it had to come to this. It was just a bad, bad decision he made. But he took a life. And it's unfortunate that two young lives are affected so negatively about this.
paragraphs.cn: Sayang at umabot sa ganito. Gumawa siya ng napakasamang desisyon. Pero kumuha siya ng buhay. Sa kasamaang palad, dalawang batang buhay ang naapektuhan nang napaka-negatibo.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Anthony did not testify. His mother took the stand during the sentencing phase, telling jurors her son was sorry. And CNN now projecting Republican Steve Hilton will advance to the November election in the California governor's race against Democrat Xavier Becerra. Becerra, the former health secretary, finished on top with nearly 28 percent of the vote, three points ahead of Hilton, a former FOX News host who has President Trump's endorsement.
paragraphs.cn: Hindi tumestigo si Anthony. Ang kanyang ina ay humarap sa korte sa yugto ng pagpapataw ng sentensya, sinabi sa mga hurado na nagsisisi ang kanyang anak. Ngayon, hinuhulaan ng CNN na ang Republikano na si Steve Hilton ay uusad sa eleksyon ng gobernador ng California noong Nobyembre, laban sa Demokratang si Xavier Becerra. Ang dating Kalihim ng Kalusugan na si Becerra ay nangunguna sa halos 28% ng boto, tatlong puntos ang agwat sa dating host ng Fox News na si Hilton, na suportado ni Pangulong Trump.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: STEVE HILTON (R), CALIFORNIA GUBERNATORIAL CANDIDATE; Californians will have a real choice for change in November. Whether they want to keep going with the policies that have brought us to the highest poverty rate, the highest unemployment rate, the highest cost of living in the country, where they want more of that with Xavier Becerra, or whether they want to go in a new direction.
paragraphs.cn: Steve Hilton (Republikano), kandidato para sa gobernador ng California: Ang mga taga-California ay magkakaroon ng tunay na pagpipilian para sa pagbabago sa Nobyembre. Gusto ba nilang ipagpatuloy ang mga patakarang nagdudulot ng pinakamataas na antas ng kahirapan, pinakamataas na antas ng kawalan ng trabaho, pinakamataas na gastos sa pamumuhay sa ating bansa, o gusto nilang magpatuloy kasama si Xavier Becerra, o gusto nilang pumunta sa bagong direksyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I have to ask quickly: if Hilton does well, is California still going to be the corrupt election state that Republicans are saying it is?
paragraphs.cn: Kailangan kong mabilis na magtanong: Kung maganda ang performance ni Hilton, magiging corrupt election state pa rin ba ang California ayon sa mga Republikano?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: Yes. I mean, the question is how well, of course. How much can he actually make some --
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Ibig kong sabihin, ang tanong ay kung gaano siya kagaling. Gaano ba talaga siya kalaki ang makukuha -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Is there a certain threshold after which --
paragraphs.cn: Mayroon bang isang threshold na lampas doon -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- it's no longer considered corrupt if a Republican wins?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- kung manalo ang Republikano, hindi na ito ituturing na corrupt?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: Well, you know, you know what? Especially given the state, it'll depend on how the votes are counted. I feel that especially after the next --
paragraphs.cn: Well, alam mo ba? Lalo na sa estadong ito, depende ito sa kung paano mabibilang ang mga boto. Sa tingin ko, lalo na sa susunod -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But you can see why I'm asking, right? Like, if he wins, it's fine. But if he doesn't win, it's corrupt.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Pero naiintindihan mo kung bakit ko ito tinatanong, di ba? Halimbawa, kung siya ang manalo, okay lang. Pero kung hindi siya manalo, corrupt.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But if he wins, it's still corrupt, but somehow --
paragraphs.cn: Pero kung siya ang manalo, corrupt pa rin, pero kahit papaano -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Maybe, maybe. I think it's still fairly unlikely that he's going to win this race. I will also say that it's been the process in California taking so long to discover who --
paragraphs.cn: Siguro, siguro. Sa tingin ko maliit pa rin ang tsansa niyang manalo sa karerang ito. Gusto ko ring sabihin na ang proseso ng pagpili ng kandidato sa California ay masyadong matagal --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, everyone's talking about that.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, lahat ay pinag-uusapan ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: -- is going to win it. Everybody's talking about this.
paragraphs.cn: -- ay mananalo. Lahat ay pinag-uusapan ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But everyone's disagreeing on what that means.
paragraphs.cn: Pero iba-iba ang opinyon ng lahat tungkol sa kahulugan nito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That's by definition nefarious.
paragraphs.cn: Ito mismo ay masama.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: But it makes people uncomfortable with the results.
paragraphs.cn: Pero ito ay nagpapahina ng loob ng mga tao sa resulta.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I have to mention one other thing, just because the number's astounding. Tom Steyer's spending: 215 million. I'm not good at long division, so I didn't want to know how much per voter that spend was, and I can't do it. I can't do it. And someone on -- online was saying, oh, have you ever heard of effective altruism, and that this was ineffective altruism, basically.
paragraphs.cn: Kailangan ko ring banggitin ang isa pang bagay, dahil lang sa nakakagulat na bilang. Ang ginastos ni Tom Steyer: $215 milyon. Hindi ako magaling sa mahabang dibisyon, kaya ayaw kong malaman kung magkano iyon bawat botante, hindi ko rin makalkula. Hindi ko makalkula. May nagsabi online, oh, narinig mo na ba ang epektibong altruismo? At ito ay talagang hindi epektibong altruismo.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Basically, an ROI in the business term, right? You need a return on that investment. This is twice for Steyer now. He has not gotten that ROI. When is enough, enough? If I had that kind of money, I would invest it in something else. He has tried this political acumen.
paragraphs.cn: Talaga, sa terminong pangnegosyo, return on investment, tama? Kailangan mo ng return on investment. Ito na ang pangalawang pagkakataon para kay Steyer. Hindi niya nakuha ang return on investment na iyon. Kailan sapat? Kung mayroon akong ganoong karaming pera, mamumuhunan ako sa ibang lugar. Sinubukan na niya ang ganitong taktikang pampulitika.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: It has not worked out.
paragraphs.cn: Pero hindi ito nagtagumpay.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. I was about to say --
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Sasabihin ko sana --

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Voters have said that.
paragraphs.cn: Sinabi na iyon ng mga botante.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The voters have rejected it.
paragraphs.cn: Tinanggihan na ito ng mga botante.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Especially with the billionaire. When the billionaire class. We've got AOC and others that have talked about the billionaire class, and we have somebody here spending his own money like this, it seems like a waste where it could be invested in other.
paragraphs.cn: Lalo na ang bilyonaryong iyon. Pagdating sa klase ng mga bilyonaryo, mayroon tayong AOC at iba pang nag-usap tungkol sa klase ng mga bilyonaryo, at ngayon may taong gumagastos ng sarili niyang pera nang ganito, tila ito ay isang pag-aaksaya, ang perang ito ay maaaring ininvest sa ibang lugar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, it's been a wild race coming from last year to all of a sudden have Becerra at the front.
paragraphs.cn: Well, simula noong nakaraang taon, ang kampanyang ito ay naging matindi, biglang nanguna si Becerra.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We couldn't have seen that then.
paragraphs.cn: Hindi namin inaasahan iyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, two controversial to win? So, the Colorado frontrunner for governor claims he killed a man when he was 7 years old. I have more stories like that from him. Plus, A.I. companies are rushing to the stock market. Is it the next tech boom or is the A.I. bubble about to burst? And for viewers watching us from right here in New York City, Knicks versus Spurs again tonight at the Garden. Good morning.
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon, pagkatapos ng break sa CNN This Morning, dalawang kontrobersyal na tagumpay? Ang nangunguna sa gubernatorial race ng Colorado ay nagsabing pumatay siya ng isang tao noong siya ay 7 taong gulang. Marami pa akong kwento tungkol sa kanya. Bukod dito, ang mga AI company ay nagmamadaling mag-IPO. Ito ba ang susunod na tech boom, o sasabog na ang AI bubble? Para sa mga manonood namin sa New York City, ngayong gabi ang Knicks ay maglalaro muli laban sa Spurs sa Garden. Magandang umaga.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, a bunch of IPOs in the A.I. sector are about to hit Wall Street after OpenAI announced on Monday it filed for an initial public offering, an IPO. The three biggest A.I. companies are now in line to go public, as well. That includes Anthropic and SpaceX. A fourth, Perplexity, is expected to file in 2028.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, pagkatapos ng anunsyo ng OpenAI noong Lunes na mag-file ng IPO, isang grupo ng mga AI IPO ang darating sa Wall Street. Tatlo sa pinakamalaking AI company ay malapit nang mag-IPO, kabilang ang Anthropic at SpaceX. Ang ikaapat na kumpanya, Perplexity, ay inaasahang mag-file sa 2028.

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: We're talking about it. Where the American people can benefit from the success of A.I. And by doing that, they're going to like it better. Because we're leading China. We're leading everybody in the world with A.I., and we want to keep it that way. It's like you make them a partnership in this revolution. It would be a beautiful thing.
paragraphs.cn: Pinag-uusapan natin ito. Ang mga Amerikano ay maaaring makinabang mula sa tagumpay ng AI. Sa paggawa nito, mas magugustuhan nila ito. Dahil tayo ay nangunguna sa China sa AI, nangunguna sa lahat ng bansa sa mundo, gusto nating manatili sa ganoong estado. Ito ay tulad ng paggawa sa kanila na kasosyo sa rebolusyong ito. Ito ay magiging isang magandang bagay.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, investors who get in early could make a fortune. But has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself? Madison Mills joins the group chat. She's the senior A.I. reporter at Axios. You guys have done some fantastic reporting on this industry. Just to show people the amount of money at stake. When you look at OpenAI and SpaceX and Anthropic, there were 3.5 trillion. We dug around, and we found out that's, like, as much money as, you know, France. Like, OK? So, we're dealing with a lot of money.
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon, ang mga namumuhunan na pumasok nang maaga ay maaaring kumita ng malaki. Ngunit ang AI boom ba ay nauuna na? Sumali si Madison Mills sa chat. Siya ay senior AI reporter ng Axios. Gumawa kayo ng ilang magagandang coverage tungkol sa industriyang ito. Para lang ipakita sa mga tao ang laki ng perang kasangkot. Kapag tiningnan mo ang OpenAI, SpaceX, at Anthropic, ang kabuuang halaga ay $3.5 trilyon. Naghukay kami at nalaman na ito ay katumbas ng ekonomiya ng France, gets niyo? Kaya nakikipag-usap tayo sa napakalaking pera.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But is it a bubble? And I ask because we've got some people on TikTok who think that it is. I'm going to play for you, the finance experts online.
paragraphs.cn: Ngunit ito ba ay bubble? Itanong ko ito dahil may mga tao sa TikTok na nag-iisip na ito ay bubble. I-play ko para sa inyo ang sinasabi ng financial expert online.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: A.I. companies are now trying to save themselves by applying for IPOs.
paragraphs.cn: Ang mga AI company ngayon ay sinusubukang iligtas ang kanilang sarili sa pamamagitan ng pag-file ng IPO.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop and that these insiders and institutional investors know that they better cash out now, or they may not ever get the chance.
paragraphs.cn: Ang OpenAI at Anthropic ay nagpaplanong mag-IPO bago matapos ang taong ito, na nagpapakita sa akin na ang AI bubble ay sasabog na, at ang mga insider at institutional investors na ito ay alam na mas mabuting mag-cash out na sila ngayon, o baka wala nang pagkakataon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Why is this the word on the street?
paragraphs.cn: Bakit ito ay anecdotal?

paragraphs.speaker: Madison Mills
paragraphs.en: Yes, I would push back on a lot of the financial finfluencing that we hear about on finance.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, sasalungat ako sa maraming sinasabi ng mga financial influencer na naririnig natin sa finance.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Finfluencing. You said it. Go on.
paragraphs.cn: Financial influencer. Tama ka. Sige.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: Yes. But -- but just because companies are IPOing doesn't necessarily mean that the bubble is going to happen this Friday when SpaceX lists on the
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Pero -- -- hindi ibig sabihin na dahil lang nag-IPO ang isang kumpanya ay tiyak na may bubble kapag nag-IPO ang SpaceX ngayong Biyernes.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: Right? But the concept of the bubble is this idea that valuations are completely divorced from reality: the underlying business fundamentals, the things that we would learn about in journalism and business school --
paragraphs.cn: Tama? Pero ang konsepto ng bubble ay ang valuation ay ganap na hiwalay sa realidad: hiwalay sa mga bagay na natututunan natin sa balita at sa business school -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: -- that make a company profitable, right?
paragraphs.cn: -- -- ang mga fundamentals na nagpapakita ng kita ng kumpanya, tama?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, the street phrase would be they're high on their own supply?
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, ang jargon ay sila ay self-producing at self-selling?

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. And that's very clear with a company like SpaceX. Their expected valuation is $1.8 trillion. They're trading at about 90 times their sales -- not their profits, just their sales -- as of this Friday, when they're expected to list. So, that is an example of a company you might look at and say, not sure if the valuation is high.
paragraphs.cn: Tama. Ang mga kumpanyang tulad ng SpaceX ay halata. Ang inaasahang valuation nila ay $1.8 trilyon. Sa oras na mag-IPO sila ngayong Biyernes, ang presyo ng kanilang shares ay mga 90 beses ng kanilang sales -- -- hindi profit, kundi sales lang. Kaya, ito ay isang halimbawa kung saan maaari mong sabihin na hindi sigurado kung overvalued ba ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I know. There's also this thing I've been learning about circular financing.
paragraphs.cn: Alam ko. May nalaman din ako tungkol sa isang bagay na tinatawag na circular financing.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, I'm understanding this as, you use money from one A.I. company to do work in your A.I. company. and then another A.I. company gets work from you. It's like the same money going around and around. Do I have that right?
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, ang pagkakaintindi ko ay, kukuha ka ng pera mula sa isang AI company para sa trabaho ng iyong AI company, at pagkatapos ay kukuha naman ng trabaho mula sa iyo ang isa pang AI company. Parang iisang pera lang ang umiikot. Tama ba ang pagkakaintindi ko?

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: Absolutely. That's perfect. And part of --
paragraphs.cn: Tama. Maganda ang pagkakasabi mo. At bahagi -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm ready to finfluence.
paragraphs.cn: Handa na akong maging financial influencer.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: You're ready to finfluence. And more than that, you can be the CFO of an A.I. company. You know? But part of the problem with that is that Anthropic can -- or OpenAI, any of the A.I. labs, can book that as revenue. And that feeds into that valuation number that we're getting. But that revenue could also just be money that they are getting from Google, that then they're giving back to Google in the form of compute capacity. And then Google has that compute capacity and is giving it over to another A.I. lab.
paragraphs.cn: Handa ka nang maging financial influencer. Hindi lang iyon, maaari ka pang maging CFO ng isang AI company, alam mo ba? Pero bahagi ng problema ay ang Anthropic o OpenAI, o anumang AI lab, ay maaaring ituring ang perang iyon bilang revenue. At ito ay nakakaapekto sa valuation numbers na nakikita natin. Pero ang revenue na iyon ay maaaring pera lang na kinuha nila mula sa Google, at pagkatapos ay ibinalik nila sa Google bilang computing power. At pagkatapos ay may computing power ang Google, at ibibigay ito sa isa pang AI lab.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: And so, it's all very circular right now. And that's the concern.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, ngayon ang lahat ng ito ay napaka-cyclical. Ito ang nakababahala.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it's giving shell game. I'm going to listen to you, because I believe that this market is the future. I understand that, but I think people rightly have questions. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Parang naglalaro lang ito. Makikinig ako sa iyo dahil naniniwala ako na ang market na ito ang kinabukasan. Naiintindihan ko iyon, pero sa tingin ko makatuwiran lang na magkaroon ng mga tanong ang mga tao.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to listen to you because I believe that this market is the future. I understand that. But I think people, rightly, have questions. There are a ton of people who are pushing back on A.I. right now. You've got some local municipalities super upset about data centers coming near them. Yesterday we played Brad Paisley in Nashville, upset there might be a data center next to the zoo.
paragraphs.cn: Makikinig ako sa iyo dahil naniniwala ako na ang market na ito ang kinabukasan. Naiintindihan ko iyon. Pero sa tingin ko makatuwiran lang na magkaroon ng mga tanong ang mga tao. Maraming tao ngayon ang lumalaban sa artificial intelligence. Ang ilang lokal na pamahalaan ay hindi masaya na magkaroon ng data center malapit sa kanila. Kahapon ay pinalabas namin ang programa ni Brad Paisley sa Nashville, kung saan ang mga tao ay hindi masaya na maaaring magtayo ng data center sa tabi ng zoo.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Here at CNN I want to disclose, we're currently suing Perplexity, alleging that the company is unlawfully copying and sharing CNN content in its summaries. I've also heard of people complaining about the summaries as well. How does that figure in to how investors look at this industry, which is facing massive blowback? MADISON MILLS, SENIOR A.I. REPORTER, "AXIOS": Yes, this A.I. hate wave that we're seeing could be a real problem for these A.I.
paragraphs.cn: Sa CNN, gusto kong ibunyag na kasalukuyan naming kinasuhan ang Perplexity, na inaakusahan ang kumpanya na ilegal na kinokopya at ibinabahagi ang nilalaman ng CNN sa kanilang mga buod. Nakakarinig din ako ng mga reklamo tungkol sa mga buod na ito. Paano ito nakakaapekto sa mga investor na tumitingin sa industriyang ito na nakakaranas ng malaking backlash? Si Madison Mills, senior AI reporter ng Axios: Oo, ang alon ng pagkamuhi sa AI na nakikita natin ay maaaring maging tunay na problema para sa mga kumpanyang ito ng AI.

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: companies because they need something called compute that's like the currency, the energy fueling the A.I. race. If the A.I. labs can't get their data centers, then they can't get the compute that they need to service your queries.
paragraphs.cn: Dahil kailangan nila ang tinatawag na compute, na parang pera, ang enerhiya na nagtutulak sa karera ng AI. Kung hindi makakuha ng data center ang mga AI lab, hindi nila makukuha ang compute na kailangan para iproseso ang mga query ng user.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: So, it could be a big problem. And we're seeing this data center pushback really hitting a fever pitch.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, ito ay maaaring maging malaking problema. Nakikita natin na ang pagtutol sa mga data center na ito ay talagang umabot na sa sukdulan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, because some of them are going -- are supposed to be coming up with their own way to power -- to sort of self-power the data centers.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, dahil ang ilan sa mga data center na ito ay dapat na makabuo ng sarili nilang kuryente -- sa isang paraan ay self-powered.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But, like, people don't want them. And I was thinking, when I look at how people talk about A.I., for instance, we played the president earlier and he said, "we're talking about it where the American people can benefit from the success of A.I. They're going to like it better because we're leading China, we're leading everyone in the world. We want to keep it that way." And I felt like, if you took away the word A.I. and put "globalization," we've heard this before.
paragraphs.cn: Pero, ayaw sila ng mga tao. Iniisip ko, kapag nakikita ko kung paano pinag-uusapan ng mga tao ang AI, tulad ng pinalabas natin kanina ang talumpati ng presidente, sinabi niya: 'Pinag-uusapan natin kung paano mapapakinabangan ng mga Amerikano ang tagumpay ng AI. Mas magugustuhan nila ito dahil nauuna tayo sa China, nauuna sa lahat ng bansa sa mundo. Gusto nating manatili sa ganoong estado.' Sa tingin ko, kung papalitan natin ang salitang 'AI' ng 'globalization', narinig na natin ito dati.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And I wonder if that is contributing to what you call the hate wave.
paragraphs.cn: Gusto kong malaman kung ito ba ang dahilan ng tinatawag mong 'alon ng pagkamuhi'.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: People feeling like, I'm being told something's going to make my life better. And my experience in the past is that that isn't -- doesn't play out the way politicians say it will.
paragraphs.cn: Pakiramdam ng mga tao, may nagsasabi sa akin na may bagay na magpapabuti sa aking buhay, at ang aking karanasan noon ay hindi ito natutupad tulad ng sinasabi ng mga politiko.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: I mean, I would push back on that too because the overwhelming research shows that people are not happy about A.I. There's this YouGov poll people cite all the time that showed very clear bipartisan support against A.I. across the aisle.
paragraphs.cn: Ang ibig kong sabihin, sasalungat din ako diyan, dahil ang karamihan sa mga pag-aaral ay nagpapakita na ang mga tao ay hindi nasisiyahan sa AI. Ang madalas na binabanggit na YouGov poll ay nagpapakita na ang pagtutol sa AI mula sa parehong partido ay napakalinaw, lumalampas sa mga hangganan ng partido.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: And we're seeing that play out with the data center pushback that you had mentioned. I also do think part of the challenge is that these models are getting better so much faster than anything we've seen.
paragraphs.cn: Nakikita natin na ang pagtutol sa data center na binanggit mo ay nagaganap. Sa tingin ko rin, bahagi ng hamon ay ang bilis ng pagpapabuti ng mga modelong ito ay mas mabilis kaysa sa anumang nakita natin dati.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Anthropic releasing a safer work mythos.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Naglabas ang Anthropic ng isang mas ligtas na work myth.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Which is still scary.
paragraphs.cn: Nakakatakot pa rin ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: After saying that they were so worried about it they only brought it to the government and certain companies.
paragraphs.cn: Matapos sabihin na labis silang nag-aalala tungkol dito, ibinigay lamang nila ito sa gobyerno at sa ilang kumpanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.en: Exactly. And because of that, I think that people haven't necessarily seen a lot of the benefits of A.I. because it's getting better so much faster. And so people are, you know, hearing about a data center in their backyard, they're hearing A.I. CEOs say that it could replace their jobs. And, by the way, you haven't really felt the positive use cases of it yet because it's getting more technical more quickly. So, I think that creates kind of a challenging soup for people. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Tama. Dahil dito, sa tingin ko hindi talaga nakikita ng mga tao ang maraming benepisyo ng artificial intelligence, dahil napakabilis nitong umunlad. Kaya, naririnig ng mga tao na magkakaroon ng data center sa kanilang bakuran, naririnig ang AI CEO na nagsasabing maaari nitong palitan ang kanilang trabaho. At, siyempre, hindi pa nila talaga nararanasan ang mga positibong gamit nito dahil napakabilis ng pag-unlad ng teknolohiya. Kaya, sa tingin ko ito ay lumilikha ng isang mahirap na sitwasyon para sa mga tao.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. We'll see what happens when people are ringing that bell on Wall Street. Madison Mills, thank you so much for being here. Always love to have you. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, the World Cup kicks off tomorrow. But with sky high ticket prices, problems with immigration, is this going to be the World Cup of chaos? Plus, Nancy Mace broke with President Trump over the Epstein files. Is that what costs her a shot at the governor's mansion in South Carolina, or was it something else?
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Abangan natin kung ano ang mangyayari kapag tumunog na ang kampana sa Wall Street. Madison Mills, maraming salamat sa iyong pagdating. Lagi kaming natutuwa kapag nandito ka. Susunod sa CNN This Morning, magsisimula na ang World Cup bukas. Ngunit mataas ang presyo ng tiket, maraming isyu sa imigrasyon, magiging magulo kaya ang World Cup na ito? At saka, si Nancy Mace ay humiwalay kay Pangulong Trump dahil sa mga Epstein files. Nawalan na ba siya ng pagkakataong maging gobernador ng South Carolina, o may iba pang dahilan?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, the World Cup kicks off tomorrow. I know, I said it. It kicks off across North America. But are the off the field headlines threatening to overshadow the biggest tournament in the world? So, there was a Somali referee turned away by U.S. Customs and Border Patrol in Miami when he arrived for his World Cup assignment. And he received a hero's welcome when he returned to Somalia. The Trump administration tells CNN that vetting uncovered derogatory information. They did not go into further detail.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, magsisimula na ang World Cup bukas. Alam ko, nasabi ko na. Magaganap ito sa iba't ibang bahagi ng North America. Ngunit ang mga balita sa labas ng laro ba ay magbabanta sa atensyon na dapat ay para sa pinakamalaking sporting event sa mundo? Isang Somali referee ang tinanggihan ng U.S. Customs and Border Protection sa Miami, dapat sana siya ay mag-officiate sa World Cup. Bumalik siya sa Somalia na sinalubong ng bayani. Sinabi ng administrasyong Trump sa CNN na ang background check ay nagpakita ng hindi kanais-nais na impormasyon, ngunit walang ibang detalye.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, the ref told "The New York Times," quote, "I'm just simply a referee who's trying to live his dream." Fans from Scotland said their permits were withdrawn at the final moment. Teams from Senegal, Uzbekistan were treated, quote, "like criminals," according to critics. And a player from Iraq was questioned for seven hours upon arrival in Chicago.
paragraphs.cn: Sinabi ng referee sa New York Times: "Ako ay isang referee lamang na nagsisikap na makamit ang kanyang pangarap." Ang mga fans mula sa Scotland ay nagsabing ang kanilang mga permit ay binawi sa huling sandali. Sinasabi ng mga kritiko na ang mga koponan mula Senegal at Uzbekistan ay trinato "parang mga kriminal." Isang manlalaro mula sa Iraq ay inusisa ng pitong oras pagdating sa Chicago.

paragraphs.speaker: Ian Wright
paragraphs.en: Every few hours it's another story. Another story about fans denied, players denied, officials denied, journalists denied. Now refs. You know something? I'm laughing, but it's not funny. It's actually not funny. Something has to be said. It's expensive tickets. Most expensive tickets ever. Know what? This is the World Cup. This is a World Cup of chaos.
paragraphs.cn: Bawat ilang oras ay may bagong kuwento. Isa na namang kuwento tungkol sa mga fan na tinanggihan, mga manlalaro na tinanggihan, mga opisyal na tinanggihan, mga mamamahayag na tinanggihan. Ngayon naman ay ang referee. Alam mo? Tumatawa ako, pero hindi ito nakakatawa. Sa totoo lang, hindi talaga nakakatawa. Kailangang may sabihin. Mahal ang mga tiket, pinakamahal sa kasaysayan. Alam mo? Ito ang World Cup. Ito ay isang magulong World Cup.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Joining us in the group chat, Dan Courtemanche. He's the executive vice president and chief communications officer for Major League Soccer. Did I say your name right? I don't want your mom being mad at me.
paragraphs.cn: Sumali sa aming group chat si Dan Courtemanche, Executive Vice President at Chief Communications Officer ng Major League Soccer. Nasabi ko ba nang tama ang pangalan mo? Ayokong magalit ang nanay mo sa akin.

paragraphs.speaker: Dan Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: You did. Thank you. It's terrific to be here. Really appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: Tama ka. Salamat. Masaya akong nandito. Maraming salamat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, I wanted to talk to you about this for a couple of reasons. One, your team sent over some information here, and I was noting that there is a record number of MLS players, 45, who represent countries in this, right?
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, gusto kitang kausapin tungkol dito, sa ilang kadahilanan. Una, may ipinadala ang iyong team na impormasyon, at napansin ko na may record na 45 MLS players na kumakatawan sa kani-kanilang bansa, tama ba?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And that you've also got -- done a lot of work through your academies to be a place to recruit people who will want to play here. Does this -- these headlines, do they kind of undermine that message that MLS has been so good, frankly, at cultivating over the last decade?
paragraphs.cn: At marami rin kayong ginagawa sa pamamagitan ng youth academy system, na naging lugar para sa pangangalap ng mga gustong maglaro dito. Napapahina ba ng mga balitang ito ang imahe na maingat na itinayo ng MLS sa nakaraang dekada?

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Yes, I think what we're going to see is some of those headlines tomorrow when the World Cup opens in Mexico City, then certainly in North America and Canada and the United States on Friday. It's going to shift from some of those headlines, the World Cup of chaos, to celebration, to pageantry, to a party.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, sa tingin ko bukas kapag magbubukas ang World Cup sa Mexico City, makakakita tayo ng ilan sa mga balitang ito, siyempre sa Biyernes sa North America, Canada at Estados Unidos. Ang focus ay lilipat mula sa mga balitang 'magulong World Cup' patungo sa pagdiriwang, karangyaan, at party.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I'm saying, for the players, is this irritating, scary, frustrating? The whole point is international cooperation.
paragraphs.cn: Pero gusto kong sabihin, para sa mga manlalaro, ito ba ay nakakainis, nakakatakot, nakakadismaya? Ang buong kahulugan ay internasyonal na kooperasyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's not feeling welcoming.
paragraphs.cn: Ito ay parang hindi gaanong tinatanggap.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: So, I hear you. You know, ultimately, that's a question for the players. It's not something that we've seen. And in Major League Soccer, we have players from 78 different countries. And we've been around for 31 seasons. And we haven't experienced that, challenges with visa issues or anything. But I understand where you're coming from. We want to be welcoming to all the players. We want to see them have a wonderful time here in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, narinig ko ang iyong opinyon. Alam mo, sa huli, ito ay problema ng mga manlalaro. Hindi namin nakikita ang sitwasyong ito. Sa Major League Soccer, mayroon kaming mga manlalaro mula sa 78 iba't ibang bansa. Nag-opera na kami ng 31 season. Hindi kami nakaranas ng mga hamon tulad ng mga isyu sa visa. Ngunit naiintindihan ko ang iyong iniisip. Nais naming tanggapin ang lahat ng manlalaro. Nais naming makita silang magkaroon ng magandang oras sa Estados Unidos, Canada, at Mexico.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And a lot of your incoming star players, over the last couple of years, coming to the U.S., they're coming from abroad. It's finally a place people are starting to come to and want to play, right?
paragraphs.cn: Sa nakaraang ilang taon, maraming darating na star player ang pumunta sa Estados Unidos, galing sila sa ibang bansa. Sa wakas, ito ay naging lugar na pinapangarap ng mga tao at gustong puntahan para maglaro, tama ba?

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Most definitely. The greatest of all time. Lionel Messi, by the way, scored last night in Auburn, Alabama, in a friendly against Iceland. You'll see him on Tuesday in Kansas City against Algeria. I hope he scores a lot of goals because then you can watch him playing for Inter Miami each and every weekend in Major League Soccer.
paragraphs.cn: Walang duda. Ang pinakadakilang manlalaro sa kasaysayan. Si Lionel Messi ay umiskor kagabi sa warm-up match laban sa Iceland sa Auburn, Alabama. Makikita mo siya sa Martes sa Kansas City laban sa Algeria. Sana marami siyang ma-iskor para mapanood mo siya tuwing weekend sa MLS para sa Inter Miami.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, what I loved about that person who spoke at the end of our introduction is he goes, oh, I laugh because, you know, it's sort of -- sort of sad. And then he tied all of that to ticket prices. And I thought that was really fascinating, that people are also looking at this in the context of the cost of the ticket. The average get in price for a World Cup match right now, roughly $600 for the opening round of the tournament. And people are complaining about FIFA's pricing scheme.
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon, nagustuhan ko ang sinabi ng taong iyon sa pagtatapos ng aming introduksyon, sabi niya, oh, natawa ako, dahil alam mo, medyo -- medyo nakakalungkot. Tapos iniugnay niya ito sa presyo ng tiket. Sa tingin ko ito ay kawili-wili, na tinitingnan din ng mga tao ito mula sa pananaw ng presyo ng tiket. Ang average na presyo ng pagpasok sa World Cup ngayon, sa group stage, ay mga 600 dolyar. Nagrereklamo ang mga tao tungkol sa pricing scheme ng FIFA.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And right now, if I want, I can still get a ticket to USA Paraguay. It's not sold out, and it should be.
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon, kung gusto ko, makakabili pa rin ako ng tiket para sa USA vs Paraguay. Hindi pa sold out ang laban, pero dapat sold out na.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Yes, I can tell you, I'm going to be there for the U.S.- Paraguay game. I certainly anticipate it's going to be sold out. I got to tell you, there's unprecedented demand for the World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, masasabi ko sa iyo, manonood ako ng USA vs Paraguay. Inaasahan ko na tiyak na magiging sold out ito. Kailangan kong sabihin sa iyo, ang demand para sa World Cup ay hindi pa naganap.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Then why isn't it sold out?
paragraphs.cn: Bakit hindi pa sold out?

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Much like you'll see from my Knicks tonight, where the get-in price is about $15,000 for lower level seating. Now, that said, I think you're going to see the pageantry, the celebration throughout the World Cup. But I understand where people are coming from. Also, you have to remind people that FIFA is actually a non-profit. So, all that money goes back to the 211 FIFA nations to build the sport, especially in some underdeveloped countries.
paragraphs.cn: Tulad ng makikita mo mamaya sa laro ng aking Knicks, ang presyo ng pagpasok sa lower level ay mga 15,000 dolyar. Sa kabila nito, sa tingin ko makikita mo ang kasiyahan at pagdiriwang sa panahon ng World Cup. Ngunit naiintindihan ko ang iniisip ng mga tao. At kailangan mong ipaalala sa mga tao na ang FIFA ay isang non-profit organization. Kaya, lahat ng pera ay ibabalik sa 211 member countries ng FIFA para sa pagpapaunlad ng football, lalo na sa mga hindi gaanong maunlad na bansa.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, I'm glad you brought that up because we did have a California lawmaker, Representative Sydney Kamlager-Dove. She was on the show Friday. She said something I didn't expect to hear related to ticket prices and what her concerns were. Here's what she had to say. REP. SYDNEY KAMLAGER-DOVE (D-CA): The issue is dynamic pricing. The other issue is, you buy a ticket, you don't know where that seat is going to be. You're spending over $1,000.
paragraphs.cn: Okay, natutuwa akong binanggit mo iyon, dahil mayroon kaming isang kongresista mula sa California, si Representative Sydney Kamlager-Dove. Sumali siya sa programa noong Biyernes. Sinabi niya ang ilang bagay na hindi ko inaasahan na maririnig, tungkol sa presyo ng tiket at kanyang mga alalahanin. Narito ang kanyang sinabi. Representative Sydney Kamlager-Dove (D-CA): Ang problema ay dynamic pricing. Ang isa pang problema ay, bumili ka ng tiket, hindi mo alam kung saan ang upuan mo. Gagastos ka ng higit sa 1,000 dolyar.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And finally you got the -- you get the seat and you're behind a post and you can't see anything. We want to know why ticket prices are changing so dramatically from city to city. I want to know why cities aren't getting any financial support from the World Cup to actually put on the games, because World Cup is getting everything, concessions, merchandise, parking, tickets.
paragraphs.cn: Sa huli, makukuha mo ang upuan, pero nasa likod ng poste, wala kang makita. Gusto naming malaman kung bakit malaki ang pagkakaiba ng presyo ng tiket sa iba't ibang lungsod. Gusto kong malaman kung bakit ang mga lungsod ay walang natatanggap na pinansyal na suporta mula sa World Cup para mag-host ng mga laban, dahil kinukuha ng World Cup ang lahat: franchises, merchandise, parking, tickets.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: That was just a long list. Can you answer any of it?
paragraphs.cn: Maraming tanong ito. Masasagot mo ba ang alinman sa mga ito?

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Well, I can tell you, look, we're fortunate, five of the stadiums out of the 13 markets in the United States and Canada are Major League Soccer stadiums. And I can tell you that nobody will be sitting behind posts during the World Cup games in those MLS stadiums. So, look, she brings up some valid points. She's clearly an expert on it. But we think it's going to pivot again from some of the challenges that we've seen in the media to the celebration.
paragraphs.cn: Well, masasabi ko sa iyo na masuwerte tayo na 5 sa 13 stadium sa US at Canada ay mga MLS stadium. Masasabi ko sa iyo na sa panahon ng World Cup, walang uupo sa likod ng mga poste sa mga MLS stadium na iyon. Nagbigay siya ng ilang makatwirang punto, at malinaw na eksperto siya rito. Ngunit sa tingin namin, ang focus ay lilipat mula sa ilang hamon na nakikita ng media patungo sa pagdiriwang.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: And ultimately goals scored by hopefully the United States national team and they make a deep run in the tournament.
paragraphs.cn: Sa huli, sana ay makapuntos ang US national team at umabot nang malayo sa torneo.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. I think, fundamentally, nobody is arguing that it won't be good. They'll be arguing about whether or not they got to participate.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Sa tingin ko, walang magtatanong na ito ay magiging mabuti. Ang pagtatalo nila ay kung makakasali sila.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Yes. You know, and I -- you know, it's also a good reminder that FIFA actually had $60 tickets available for those passionate supporters who registered early. So, they had some affordable prices. But I certainly understand you. And for us, you also have to look at, there'll be nearly 80 fan festivals throughout the different markets. Twenty of our MLS clubs are going to have soccer celebrations. So, you're providing free accessibility to fans who may not be able to go to the games.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Alam mo, ito rin ay paalala na ang FIFA ay nag-aalok ng $60 na ticket para sa mga masugid na tagahanga na nagparehistro nang maaga. Kaya, mayroon silang mga abot-kayang presyo. Ngunit naiintindihan kita. Para sa atin, makikita mo rin na magkakaroon ng halos 80 fan festival sa iba't ibang market. Ang ating 20 MLS club ay magdaraos ng football celebrations. Para sa mga hindi makapunta sa stadium, libre silang makakasali.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Well, thank you, Dan, so much for being here. Good luck. I know this is your Super Bowl. That's what we say here in the U.S. instead of soccer.
paragraphs.cn: Sige. Maraming salamat sa pagpunta rito, Dan. Good luck. Alam ko ito ang iyong Super Bowl -- -- ganyan ang sinasabi natin sa US, hindi football.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Hey, I'm a big -- I'm a University of Georgia graduate. I love gridiron football. Come on.
paragraphs.cn: Hoy, ako ay isang matinding -- -- ako ay graduate ng University of Georgia. Mahal ko ang American football. Talaga.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, OK. OK. All right. Well, thank you for being here. Appreciate your time.
paragraphs.cn: Oh, sige. Sige. Well. Salamat sa pagpunta rito. Salamat sa iyong oras.

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.en: Really appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: Maraming salamat.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Next on CNN THIS MORNING, Congresswoman Nancy Mace gets crushed in the South Carolina governor's race. Did going against Trump cost her the election? But first.
paragraphs.cn: Susunod sa CNN This Morning, si Congresswoman Nancy Mace ay natalo nang husto sa South Carolina governor's race. Ang pagtutol ba kay Trump ang dahilan ng kanyang pagkatalo? Pero una.

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: If you're elected governor, would you continue to perform exorcisms while governor?
paragraphs.cn: Kung ikaw ay mahalal na gobernador, magpapatuloy ka ba sa paggawa ng exorcism sa iyong termino?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, controversial candidate leading in the polls in Colorado. Will the strange headlines sway voters?
paragraphs.cn: Oo, ang kontrobersyal na kandidato ng Colorado ay nangunguna sa mga poll. Makakaapekto ba ang mga kakaibang headline na ito sa mga botante?

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.en: Your campaign website falsely claimed that you had rescued more than 45,000 women and children. You once called in a U.S. military airstrike that killed 70 ISIS fighters. You perform exorcisms, commanding demons to come out of people. Do you understand why so many of your critics think you're a con man? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: Ang iyong campaign website ay maling nagsasabing iniligtas mo ang mahigit 45,000 kababaihan at bata. Tumawag ka ng US airstrike na pumatay ng 70 ISIS fighters. Nagsagawa ka ng exorcism, inutusan ang mga demonyo na lumabas sa mga tao. Naiintindihan mo ba kung bakit maraming kritiko ang itinuturing kang sinungaling? Victor Marx (Republican),

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that was a wild ride. And it didn't stop there. That reporter in Colorado was interviewing a Republican frontrunner for governor, Victor Marx. He's a Marine Corps veteran. He runs a faith- based nonprofit. But he's made a lot of outlandish claims. And it does not end with exorcisms and rescuing women and children from slavery. He also says he killed a man when he was a child.
paragraphs.cn: Well, iyon ay isang nakakabaliw na usapan. At hindi pa tapos. Ang reporter sa Colorado ay iniinterbyu ang frontrunner sa Republican gubernatorial race na si Victor Marx. Siya ay isang Marine veteran. Nagpapatakbo siya ng isang faith-based nonprofit. Pero marami siyang sinasabing kakaiba. At hindi lang exorcism at pagliligtas ng kababaihan at bata mula sa pang-aalipin. Sinabi rin niya na pumatay siya ng tao noong bata pa siya.

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.en: Your claim that your abusive stepfather forced you to kill a man when you were seven years old, is that the only person you've ever killed? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: Sinasabi mong pinilit ka ng iyong stepfather na pumatay ng isang tao noong pitong taong gulang ka, iyon ba ang tanging tao na pinatay mo? Victor Marx (Republican),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.en: Well, I would say, as a -- as a child, yes. But I've been in other situations where, you know, possibly people or persons died.
paragraphs.cn: Um, sasabihin ko, bilang isang bata, oo. Pero nakaranas din ako ng ibang sitwasyon, alam mo, kung saan maaaring may namatay na tao o mga tao.

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
paragraphs.cn: Sa tingin mo ba pumatay ka ng tao noong adulto ka na?

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.en: Killing somebody is a pretty weighty thing. So, I'm just asking you, how many people have you killed?
paragraphs.cn: Ang pagpatay ay isang mabigat na bagay. Kaya, tinatanong lang kita, ilang tao ang pinatay mo?

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.en: Well, if I did, I wouldn't be telling a reporter sitting here in my training center.
paragraphs.cn: Um, kung pumatay man ako, hindi ko sasabihin sa reporter na nakaupo sa aking training center.

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.en: How about voters? Would you tell voters?
paragraphs.cn: Paano ang mga botante? Sasabihin mo ba sa mga botante?

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.en: Theres no need. I don't think that's important.
paragraphs.cn: Hindi na kailangan. Sa tingin ko hindi iyon mahalaga.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this because we started the hour talking about Maine, right, and Graham Platner and being like, voters, what are they thinking? You know, like, what's happening? What will these scandals matter? And then I was seeing a lot more stories about what's happening in Colorado. Just to give you a sense of where things are. In this Republican primary right now, the fundraising, Victor Marx is at $2.7 million.
paragraphs.cn: Okay. Bumalik na ang group chat. Gusto kong pag-usapan ito dahil sa simula ng oras na ito ay pinag-uusapan natin ang Maine, tama, at si Graham Platter, ano ang iniisip ng mga botante? Alam mo, ano ang nangyayari? Ano ang magiging epekto ng mga iskandalong ito? At pagkatapos ay nakakita ako ng higit pang mga ulat tungkol sa nangyayari sa Colorado. Para maintindihan mo ang sitwasyon. Sa Republican primary na ito, sa kasalukuyan sa fundraising, si Victor Marx ay may $2.7 milyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The next closest person is at around $500,000, who is a state senator. I know you're just kind of clocking into this, Jarrett, but help me understand why he's so far ahead in the money. That means people are the donors. The people with pockets are giving to him. JARRETT STEPMAN, CONTRIBUTOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes. In fairness, that may shift a bit after this moment, seeing him not as credible candidate.
paragraphs.cn: Ang pangalawa ay may humigit-kumulang $500,000, isang state senator. Alam kong sumali ka lang sa usapan, Jarrett, pero tulungan mo akong maintindihan kung bakit siya nangunguna nang malaki sa pondo. Ibig sabihin, ang mga tao ay donors. May pera ang mayayaman na nagbibigay sa kanya. The Daily Signal contributor Jarrett Stepman: Oo. Sa totoo lang, pagkatapos makita na hindi siya isang kapani-paniwalang kandidato, maaaring magbago ang mga bagay.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, that's a shift by about a million bucks. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: Well, iyon ay halos isang milyong dolyar na agwat. Oo.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: It would -- it would have to be quite a bit.
paragraphs.cn: That would require a lot of changes.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: And, frankly, it -- Colorado, look, they haven't elected a Republican governor in that state since --
paragraphs.cn: And, frankly, Colorado, you see, they haven't elected a Republican governor since...

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
