paragraphs.en: So, it's been a long time. I think you sometimes see this in states that have gone one way for a long time. You get sometimes candidates who are not particularly credible. There's a sort of a desperation saying, this guy seems like an outsider. He's, said, look, we saw this in Maine, somebody who's an outsider, in that case actually had a huge amount of success against a political establishment.
paragraphs.cn: So, it's been a long time. I think sometimes you see this in states that have leaned one way for a long time. Sometimes there are candidates who are less credible. There's a sense of desperation that says, this guy looks like an outsider. He says, look, we saw it in Maine, an outsider, actually in that case, had great success against the political establishment.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can I tell you how far outside --
paragraphs.cn: Can I tell you how much of an outsider he is?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Just how far outside. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK"
paragraphs.cn: How much of an outsider. "Can We Talk" host Mike Leon:

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: A bit outside is probably -- (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: A little more outsider might be -- -- (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I'm going to -- I needed to be, like, what's happening with this exorcism bit? Is this an internet joke? Is this people sort of, you know, I don't know what I thought. But here's what I saw. VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: I'm going to -- -- I need to know, what's this exorcism thing? Is it an internet joke? Or are people kind of, you know, I don't know what to think. But this is what I saw. Victor Marks (Republican),

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.en: When you say I perform exorcisms.
paragraphs.cn: When you say I perform exorcisms.

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.en: Yes. You described yourself as a, quote/unquote, reluctant exorcist.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. You describe yourself as a, quote, reluctant exorcist.

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.en: Yes. Describe to me how your exorcisms work when you command demons out.
paragraphs.cn: Yes. Describe how your exorcism works, when you command the demon to leave.

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.en: Well, that's a joke. We identify the demon, like Jesus did. We asked what their name is. And then we give them commands. And they very frequently and typically will answer through the person's mind. They'll tell us what they hear. And then we destroy them, banish them to perfect judgment from Christ.
paragraphs.cn: Well, it's a joke. We identify the demon, like Jesus did. We ask their name. Then we give them commands. They often and usually answer through the person's mind. They tell us what they heard. Then we destroy them, banishing them to the perfect judgment of Christ.

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.en: If you're elected governor, would you continue to perform exorcisms while governor?
paragraphs.cn: If you are elected governor, will you continue to perform exorcisms while in office?

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.en: I would never stop praying for people.
paragraphs.cn: Hindi ako titigil sa pagdarasal para sa mga tao.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: So, Audie, remember when we were talking about candidate quality earlier in the first segment. Shout out to Kyle Clark, the reporter there, for 9News in Denver. I love watching you on social media. He's done a lot on this one.
paragraphs.cn: Kaya, Audi, naaalala mo ba noong unang bahagi ng talata na tinalakay natin ang mga katangian ng kandidato? Shoutout kay Kyle Clark, reporter ng 9News sa Denver. Gusto kitang panoorin sa social media. Marami siyang ginawa tungkol dito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: He's actually become a bit of a social media star precisely for this coverage.
paragraphs.cn: Siya ay naging isang social media star dahil sa coverage na ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: He has. Precisely for this and the pushback. He's asked different questions of different candidates to give them factual examples. And the fact that he did this, I think it truly exposes a candidate. The fact that he has 2.7, like you said, million dollars in campaign donations. It should be alarming for all of us given this guy's track record and his history and what he just said in that interview there. We're all trying to make sense of exorcisms. And this morning about a candidate, I mean, come on. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Totoo iyan. Dahil dito at sa backlash. Nagtanong siya ng iba't ibang katanungan sa iba't ibang kandidato, binigyan sila ng mga factual na halimbawa. Ginawa niya ito, at sa tingin ko ay talagang na-expose ang mga kandidato. Gaya ng sinabi mo, mayroon siyang $2.7 milyon na campaign donations. Dahil sa track record, kasaysayan, at sinabi niya sa interview, dapat itong mag-alarma sa ating lahat. Sinusubukan nating intindihin ang exorcism. At kaninang umaga tungkol sa isang kandidato, ibig kong sabihin, please. (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. But, you know what, I think I also thought, like, OK, Marines, if he's a guy talking about the war, maybe people are interested in that. If he's a guy talking about Epstein, which he is, somebody who is constantly talking about child trafficking, there are lanes within the MAGA movement that are laser focused on this topic.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Pero, alam mo, naisip ko rin, okay, Marine, kung pinag-uusapan niya ang digmaan, baka interesado ang mga tao doon. Kung pinag-uusapan niya si Epstein, gaya ng ginagawa niya, isang taong patuloy na nagsasalita tungkol sa child trafficking, sa MAGA movement, may mga paksyon na sobrang focus sa isyung ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Max Rose
paragraphs.en: Yes. Yes, I'll say this as a partizan Democrat to my Republican friends and colleagues, that's your guy. That's your nominee. They should vote for him. They should rally for him.
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Oo, bilang isang Democrat, sasabihin ko sa aking mga Republican friends at colleagues, iyan ang inyong tao. Iyan ang inyong kandidato. Dapat silang bumoto para sa kanya. Dapat silang mag-rally para sa kanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I think this is sarcasm. I think under his name it should say Max Rose being sarcastic.
paragraphs.cn: Sa tingin ko ito ay sarcastic. Sa tingin ko dapat nakasulat sa ilalim ng kanyang pangalan na si Max Rose ay nagsasarcasm.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: They should -- we should (INAUDIBLE). This is their pathway to finally take back Colorado. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Dapat silang -- -- dapat tayong (hindi marinig). Ito ang kanilang daan para mabawi ang Colorado. (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Us Democrats are so afraid of this guy. And, you know,
paragraphs.cn: Kaming mga Democrat ay sobrang takot sa taong ito. At, alam mo,

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Leon
paragraphs.en: Republicans just learned about him.
paragraphs.cn: Kakilala pa lang siya ng mga Republican.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Oh, my God. We're --
paragraphs.cn: Oh, my God. Kami -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Listen. No, OK, what you're saying, I can see you amused, but, however, here's Lauren Boebert of Colorado giving her word on why she thinks this is a decent path. REP. LAUREN BOEBERT (R-CO): So, I think right now in Colorado we need a motivator who can then lead. Motivation has to be key in any of our Republican races, especially in a non-election year. I believe that Victor Marx will get qualified people in positions. I stand ready to help him. I am not disparaging any of the other candidates.
paragraphs.cn: Makinig. Hindi, okay, ano ang sinasabi mo, sa tingin ko nakakatawa, pero, gayunpaman, ito si Lauren Boebert ng Colorado na nagpapaliwanag kung bakit sa tingin niya ito ay isang disenteng landas. Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-CO): Kaya, sa tingin ko sa Colorado ngayon, kailangan natin ng isang motivator na kayang manguna. Ang inspirasyon ay dapat maging susi sa anumang Republican campaign, lalo na sa off-year elections. Naniniwala ako na si Victor Marx ay maglalagay ng mga kwalipikadong tao sa mga posisyon. Handa akong tulungan siya. Hindi ko binabababa ang ibang mga kandidato.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I have endorsed Victor Marx, but that's not something that I've turned the volume way up on. I want candidates to be able to professionally run their own race. I do not like the hateful back and forth that I see with Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: Sinusuportahan ko si Victor Marx, pero hindi ito ang ipinagmamalaki ko. Inaasahan ko na ang mga kandidato ay magsasagawa ng kanilang kampanya nang propesyonal. Hindi ko gusto ang puno ng poot na pagtatalo sa pagitan ng mga Republikano.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Today is a day of ringing endorsements, but --
paragraphs.cn: Ngayon ay isang araw ng malalakas na pag-endorso, pero -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I said it, don't (ph) turn up the volume.
paragraphs.cn: Sabi ko, huwag (hindi marinig) lakasan ang volume.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, no, this is the -- we talked about this during the break. The imperfect messenger argument. We heard this with Trump, right?
paragraphs.cn: Hindi, hindi, ito ay -- -- ang pinag-usapan natin noong break. Ang argumento ng hindi perpektong mensahero. Narinig din natin ito kay Trump, tama?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You're an evangelical. Maybe you're like, I don't know about the marriages, I don't know about this, I don't know about that. But maybe he will put in people that will do the thing we want.
paragraphs.cn: Ikaw ay isang evangelical. Baka isipin mo, hindi ko alam ang tungkol sa kanyang marital status, hindi ko alam ito, hindi ko alam iyon. Pero baka magtalaga siya ng mga taong gagawa ng gusto natin.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: It's the outsider again. Look, I mean, in Maine, you had a guy who has a Nazi tattoo fighting the fascists. Was that a credible campaign? Maybe it wasn't.
paragraphs.cn: Ito na naman ang outsider. Tingnan mo, ang ibig kong sabihin, sa Maine, may isang lalaking may Nazi tattoo na lumalaban sa mga pasista. Iyon ba ay isang kapani-paniwalang kampanya? Marahil hindi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Maybe he needed an exorcist.
paragraphs.cn: Baka kailangan niya ng exorcist.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And maybe he should call Colorado.
paragraphs.cn: Baka dapat siyang tumawag sa Colorado.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: But again, that's also an outsider candidate that people see as different from the establishment as it is in the state. If they think things are going the wrong way, even a charismatic, let's call it, candidate like this can punch through. Yes, the other side may say, hey, we want that guy. We want to face him. But sometimes you get what you wish for and it turns up backfiring.
paragraphs.cn: Pero muli, ito ay isang outsider candidate, na iniisip ng mga tao na iba siya sa establishment ng estado. Kung sa tingin nila na ang mga bagay ay patungo sa maling direksyon, kahit isang charismatic, sabihin nating, tulad ng kandidatong ito ay maaaring sumikat. Oo, ang kabilang panig ay maaaring sabihin, hey, gusto namin ang taong iyon. Gusto naming harapin siya. Pero minsan nakuha mo ang gusto mo, at ito ay nag-backfire.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, that does backfire. I have one more thing about being an outsider. This one is about a member of Congress who had hoped to go into a new office in the next year. They're coming up short. Last night Republican Nancy Mace finished fifth in the GOP primary for South Carolina governor. She did not get the president's endorsement in the race. She believes it was because of her vote to release the Epstein files, which may have eventually led to her defeat. REP.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, iyon ay talagang magba-backfire. May isa pa akong punto tungkol sa mga outsider. Ito ay tungkol sa isang kongresista na umaasang makapasok sa bagong opisina sa susunod na taon. Sila ay nahuhuli. Kagabi, ang Republikano na si Nancy Mace ay nagtapos sa ikalima sa Republican primary para sa gobernador ng South Carolina. Hindi niya nakuha ang endorsement ng presidente sa kanyang kampanya. Sa tingin niya ito ay dahil bumoto siya para ilabas ang mga Epstein files, na maaaring nagdulot ng kanyang pagkatalo.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: NANCY MACE (R-SC): I chose to expose the abusers of children, and apparently I chose wrong if the goal was winning an election. I'm at peace with that because when a candidate is OK with corruption, when a candidate is OK with cover ups, something is broken. And that's not political opinion, that is a moral emergency.
paragraphs.cn: Nancy Mace (R-SC): Pinili kong ilantad ang mga nang-aabuso sa bata, at malinaw na kung ang layunin ay manalo sa eleksyon, mali ang pinili ko. Payapa ako dito, dahil kapag ang isang kandidato ay nagtitiis ng korapsyon, kapag ang isang kandidato ay nagtitiis ng pagtatakip, may sira. Iyon ay hindi pampulitikang pananaw, iyon ay moral na krisis.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Group chat is back. I'm going to just say it. There's been a lot of difficult headlines for Nancy Mace. Another person who is dealing with scandal and allegations. But at the same time, she is another person who talked about Epstein a lot, right? She's another person who got on the wrong side of Trump on certain issues. Is that reflected here, or is this really candidate quality?
paragraphs.cn: Bumalik na ang group chat. Diretsahan ko na. Si Nancy Mace ay maraming mahihirap na headline. Isa pang tao na nahaharap sa iskandalo at akusasyon. Pero sa parehong oras, siya ay isa pang madalas magsalita tungkol kay Epstein, tama? Sumalungat siya kay Trump sa ilang isyu. Na-reflect ba ito dito, o ito ba ay talagang isyu ng kalidad ng kandidato?

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I want to go back to something we talked about in Maine with Susan Collins, talking about the issues, and Platner as well, talking about issues to Mainers. I feel like South Carolinians didn't hear Nancy Mace talking about that. And more of those headlines and more of her getting a microphone in front of her --
paragraphs.cn: Gusto kong bumalik sa usapan natin sa Maine kasama si Susan Collins, pag-usapan ang mga isyu, at si Plattner, na kausapin ang mga tao sa Maine tungkol sa mga isyu. Sa tingin ko hindi naririnig ng mga taga-South Carolina si Nancy Mace na pinag-uusapan ang mga ito. Mas tungkol ito sa mga headline, at kapag nakuha niya ang mikropono --

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Or coming on this network and arguing with somebody and then saying something completely the opposite when she gets off camera, I think those things are what really caught up to her in this race.
paragraphs.cn: o pumunta sa network na ito para makipagtalo sa isang tao, at pagkatapos ay magsabi ng ganap na kabaligtaran pagbaba niya sa entablado, sa tingin ko iyon ang dahilan kung bakit siya talagang napapansin sa kampanyang ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I also heard she didn't do ad spend on
paragraphs.cn: Narinig ko rin na hindi siya gumagastos sa mga ad --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Like only digital. Is that wise?
paragraphs.cn: gaya ng digital ads lang. Matalino ba iyon?

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Well, I -- first of all, Donald Trump still has a stranglehold on the Republican primary. And that is why his party is getting ripped apart.
paragraphs.cn: Well, ako -- una, si Donald Trump ay mahigpit pa ring kumokontrol sa Republican primary. Iyon ang dahilan kung bakit nahahati ang kanyang partido.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: And didn't endorse her.
paragraphs.cn: At hindi siya sinusuportahan.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Because the primary base is so extreme and they are so disassociated at this point from independents and moderates nationwide. But Nancy Mace took it one step further. She decided to antagonize, not just Donald Trump, but literally everyone in her life to include just random folks at the airport. So, what you saw here was -- and politicians do this sometimes. They jump into races not to win, but to bolster their public profile for something they're after.
paragraphs.cn: Dahil ang base voters ng primary ay sobrang extreme, at sila ngayon ay lubhang hiwalay sa mga independents at moderates sa buong bansa. Pero si Nancy Mace ay lumayo pa. Nagpasya siyang lumaban, hindi lang kay Donald Trump, kundi halos lahat ng tao sa kanyang buhay, kasama ang mga random na tao sa airport. Kaya, ang nakikita mo dito ay -- minsan ginagawa ito ng mga politiko. Hindi sila tumatakbo para manalo, kundi para itaas ang kanilang public profile para sa ilang layunin.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Well, this is the kind of thing that is in my group chat because every member of the YOLO caucus, the people who limp back to Congress from their primaries, they're really upset. And they plan to spend the next couple of months antagonizing the White House. Do you think Nancy Mace will be one of those?
paragraphs.cn: Oo. Well, iyon mismo ang nasa group chat ko, dahil ang bawat miyembro ng YOLO caucus, ang mga halos hindi nakabalik sa Kongreso mula sa primary, ay sobrang hindi nasisiyahan. Plano nilang labanan ang White House sa mga susunod na buwan. Sa tingin mo ba si Nancy Mace ay magiging isa sa kanila?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: I think she's going to in her final months. I mean she's already burned all the bridges. She's -- I think the voters in her state didn't like the drama and they said, hey, we don't want that actually running the place.
paragraphs.cn: Sa tingin ko gagawin niya iyon sa mga huling buwan. Ibig kong sabihin, sinunog na niya lahat ng tulay. Siya -- sa tingin ko ang mga botante sa kanyang estado ay hindi gusto ang ganitong drama, at sinasabi nila, hey, ayaw namin ng ganyang tao na mamuno sa lugar na ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. That's a good point, you're not effective if you're at odds with everyone.
paragraphs.cn: Oo, magandang punto. Kung hindi ka kasundo ng lahat, hindi ka magiging epektibo.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: You're not effective. And you're infective (ph). And she's going to continue this. Obviously, if she's done it up to this point, I don't think she's going to stop. But I -- she's already burned the bridges with President Trump, clearly. So, what have you got to lose now?
paragraphs.cn: Hindi ka magiging epektibo, at makakahawa ka pa (tunog). Magpapatuloy siya sa ganito. Malinaw, kung ginawa niya ito hanggang ngayon, hindi ko akalain na titigil siya. Pero ako -- ganap na siyang nakipag-away kay Trump, malinaw. Kaya, ano pa ang mawawala sa iyo ngayon?

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What have you got to lose? OK, speaking of YOLO, we're going to talk about what's in our group chats on this news show. Mike Leon, what are you talking about in your group chats?
paragraphs.cn: Ano pa ang mawawala sa iyo? Well, tungkol sa YOLO, pag-uusapan natin ang laman ng group chat sa news program na ito. Mike Leon, ano ang pinag-uusapan sa group chat mo?

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Well, first off, it's always the Knicks. But I'm talking about UFC 250. One thing that is always interesting to me --
paragraphs.cn: Well, first, always the Knicks. But I'm talking about UFC 250. One thing has always interested me --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This is the UFC fight. The claws going up on the White House lawn.
paragraphs.cn: This is a UFC fight. The claws reached the White House lawn.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: The UFC Freedom 250. Unnecessary. All of this that you're seeing here in the screen here. But Trump has always used the combat sports space to talk to voters. It's something I've talked to Democrats about. Senator Ruben Gallego, who's a big boxing fan, him and I have had this conversation. Democrat --
paragraphs.cn: UFC 250. Not necessary. All this you see on the screen. But Trump has been using the combat sports arena to talk to voters. I talked to Democrats about this. Senator Ruben Gallego is a big boxing fan, and I had that conversation with him. Democrats --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And they're giving tickets to combat (INAUDIBLE).
paragraphs.cn: They are giving away tickets to the fight (inaudible).

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Correct. And -- exactly. And Trump has always used it as a vehicle, for his relationship with Tyson and the fights that he would put on in Trump -- at Taj Mahal or whatever.
paragraphs.cn: Exactly. And -- exactly. Trump has been using it as a tool, through his relationship with Tyson and the fights he hosted at Trump Taj Mahal or elsewhere.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: He would always be in this space. He's always had these relationships because those are the kind of voters that go out and vote for him. That's the thing that we've always talked about.
paragraphs.cn: He's always in this area. He's always had these relationships, because those are the voters who go out and vote for him. This is something we always talk about.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, my friend at "The Daily Signal," what's in your group chat?
paragraphs.cn: Alright, my friend at The Daily Signal, what's in your group chat?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: So, I'm going to bring up the Knicks because it's a big thing. I moved here to New York --
paragraphs.cn: So, I'm going to mention the Knicks, because this is a big deal. I moved to New York to --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Why do I even bother?
paragraphs.cn: Why do I even bother?

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: I know. Why did you even bother.
paragraphs.cn: I know. Why do you even bother.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Just change the segment.
paragraphs.cn: Let's change the subject.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: You know, it matters to me. I'm from Oakland, California. The saddest sports town in America. We've lost all of our sports teams. So, just the fact the Knicks -- look, I have a lot of New York sports fan friends who haven't seen the Knicks or the Jets, or these teams ever do anything pretty much in their life, and now they get to spend some time with their kids and enjoy this moment. It may not come again. So, you know what --
paragraphs.cn: You know, this is important to me. I'm from Oakland, California, the saddest sports city in America. We lost all our teams. So, just the Knicks -- you see, I have many New York sports fan friends who have barely seen the Knicks, Jets, or other teams do anything in their lives, and now they get to enjoy this moment with their kids. This chance may not come again. So, you know --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I love it. I love it. It's communal.
paragraphs.cn: Gusto ko ito. Gusto ko ito. Ito ay isang kolektibong karanasan.

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.en: It's communal. Its wonderful.
paragraphs.cn: Ito ay isang kolektibong karanasan. Napakaganda.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: You know it, not just the Knicks, Donald Trump lost that game for the Knicks.
paragraphs.cn: Alam mo, hindi lang ang Knicks, si Donald Trump ang nagpatalo sa Knicks sa larong iyon.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: Zero-11, 0-11 in sporting events.
paragraphs.cn: 0 panalo at 11 talo sa mga sporting event.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You're one of those people.
paragraphs.cn: Ikaw ang ganoong uri ng tao.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Stay away. He lost it for us. We -- all we were doing is winning. This guy shows up.
paragraphs.cn: Lumayo ka. Pinalo niya tayo. Tayo ay -- -- tayo ay panalo. Lumitaw ang taong ito.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, luckily, he's not going. So, all of New York is not calling him the cooler.
paragraphs.cn: Buti na lang, hindi siya pupunta. Kaya, hindi siya tinawag na jinx ng buong New York.

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.en: Fine. I thought I couldn't dislike the guy more.
paragraphs.cn: Sige. Akala ko sapat na ang pagkamuhi ko sa kanya.

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.en: I agree. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Sumasang-ayon ako. (cross talk)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You guys. Thank you so much. Thank you for waking up with us. I'm Audie Cornish, and the headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Mga kaibigan. Maraming salamat. Salamat sa pagsama sa amin sa paggising. Ako si Oti Cornish, at susunod ang balita. var CNN = CNN || {};

vocabulary.cn: manatili sa pagpili; patuloy na suportahan
vocabulary.en: to continue to support or choose someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nagpasya ang mga Democrat ng Maine na patuloy na suportahan si Graham Plattner.

vocabulary.cn: pag-flip (tumutukoy sa partidong nanalo sa upuan na dating kontrolado ng kalaban sa eleksyon)
vocabulary.en: to win a seat or office that was previously held by the opposing party
vocabulary.excerpt: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Mababawasan ba nito ang tsansa ng partido na i-flip ang Senado ngayong taglagas?

vocabulary.en: to consider something carefully, especially by comparing different factors
vocabulary.excerpt: that's the situation that we have to weigh.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Iyan ang sitwasyong kailangan nating timbangin.

vocabulary.en: to cause something to be under pressure or tension
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Magtatagal ba ang tensyonadong tigil-putukan upang matupad ng US ang pangako nito sa Iran?

vocabulary.en: to fulfill a promise or expectation; to provide something promised
vocabulary.excerpt: for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Para matupad ng US ang pangako nito sa Iran.

vocabulary.cn: 领先者；最有希望获胜者
vocabulary.en: the person or thing that is most likely to win a competition or election
vocabulary.excerpt: Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 认识一下科罗拉多州共和党州长竞选中的领先者。

vocabulary.cn: 泡沫（指资产价格脱离基本面快速上涨）
vocabulary.en: a situation in which the price of an asset rises far above its real value, often followed by a sudden collapse
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: to burst or break open suddenly, often with a sound
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Malapit nang pumutok ang artificial intelligence bubble.

vocabulary.en: to secure a nomination or qualification, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nakuha ni Graham Plattner ang nominasyon.

vocabulary.en: to finally win or achieve something, especially after a struggle
vocabulary.excerpt: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Di-nagtagal pagkatapos ma-secure ang nominasyon, ibinaling niya ang kanyang atensyon sa kanyang bagong kalaban.

vocabulary.en: lacking courage or determination; weak-willed
vocabulary.excerpt: she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Siya ay naging kasing duwag at corrupt ng establishment na kanyang pinaglilingkuran.

vocabulary.en: the group of people who have power and influence in a society or organization, often seen as conservative or resistant to change
vocabulary.excerpt: as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ang establishment na kanyang pinaglilingkuran.

vocabulary.cn: 独立的声音（指不依附政党、独立表达意见的人）
vocabulary.en: a person or entity that expresses opinions independently, not aligned with a particular party or group
vocabulary.excerpt: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 如果你是一个独立的声音，为什么你95%的时间都和唐纳德·特朗普投票一致？

vocabulary.word: have a dog in the fight
vocabulary.cn: 在竞争中有利益关系；参与其中
vocabulary.en: to have a personal stake or interest in a particular situation or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。

vocabulary.en: at risk; something that could be lost or gained
vocabulary.excerpt: what's at stake here.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ano ang nakataya dito.

vocabulary.en: to put something at risk of being lost or harmed
vocabulary.excerpt: Maybe this has jeopardized it.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Baka ito ay nanganib na.

vocabulary.cn: Lupig na tagumpay; isang panig na halalan
vocabulary.en: an easy or decisive victory, especially in an election or sports event
vocabulary.excerpt: A lot of times it can be a blowout, right?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Madalas ito ay maaaring isang lupig na tagumpay, di ba?

vocabulary.en: fundamentally; in its most important aspects
vocabulary.excerpt: we're saying the same thing in essence, right?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们本质上说的是同一件事，对吧？

vocabulary.en: difficult to defeat or overcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Susan Collins is tough to beat.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 苏珊·柯林斯很难被击败。

vocabulary.cn: Susan Collins的弱化版（指立场相似但更温和的候选人）
vocabulary.en: a candidate who is similar to Susan Collins but less extreme or less effective
vocabulary.excerpt: you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Hindi mo maaaring manalo sa puwestong ito sa pamamagitan ng pagpili ng isang mahinang bersyon ni Susan Collins.

vocabulary.cn: 激励你的基本盘（核心支持者）
vocabulary.en: to motivate and rally the core supporters of a political party or candidate
vocabulary.excerpt: You are going to beat her by energizing your base.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kailangan mong talunin siya sa pamamagitan ng pagpapasigla sa iyong base.

vocabulary.cn: 中期选举（美国总统任期中间举行的国会选举）
vocabulary.en: an election held in the middle of a president's term, typically for Congress and state offices
vocabulary.excerpt: particularly in a midterm election.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Lalo na sa midterm elections.

vocabulary.en: a direct vote by the electorate on a specific proposal or issue
vocabulary.excerpt: this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这次选举必须是对苏珊·柯林斯的公投。

vocabulary.en: to require someone to explain their actions and accept responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 她拒绝追究唐纳德·特朗普的责任。

vocabulary.en: consecutively; one after another
vocabulary.excerpt: I want to play two clips back-to-back.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Gusto kong magpatugtog ng dalawang clip nang sunud-sunod.

vocabulary.cn: malakas na suporta; malinaw na pag-endorso
vocabulary.en: a strong and enthusiastic statement of support
vocabulary.excerpt: these are not ringing endorsements.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ang mga ito ay hindi malakas na suporta.

vocabulary.en: without her consent or desire
vocabulary.excerpt: who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Sinabi niya na bumoto siya para kay Susan Collins labag sa kanyang kalooban.

vocabulary.en: the moral and ethical qualities of a person
vocabulary.excerpt: It's really about the character quality.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ito ay tungkol sa katangian ng pagkatao.

vocabulary.en: a close and strong relationship
vocabulary.excerpt: Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Si Collins at Trump, hindi malapit ang ugnayan.

vocabulary.en: a mechanism that initiates a process or event
vocabulary.excerpt: The 60 days is a trigger.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 60天是一个触发点。

vocabulary.en: formal consent or authorization by the U.S. Congress
vocabulary.excerpt: the president has to obtain congressional approval.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 总统必须获得国会批准。

vocabulary.en: found guilty of a crime by a court of law
vocabulary.excerpt: individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ang taong nahatulan ng marahas na pag-atake sa isang pulis.

vocabulary.en: the act of paying back money spent or lost
vocabulary.excerpt: should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Dapat silang may karapatang ma-reimburse ang kanilang mga legal na gastos.

vocabulary.cn: bayad sa abogado; legal na gastos
vocabulary.en: charges for legal services provided by a lawyer or law firm
vocabulary.excerpt: reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.exampleCn: I-reimburse ang kanilang mga legal na gastos.

vocabulary.en: reductions in spending, especially in government budgets
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nag-aalala tungkol sa pagbawas ng Medicaid.

vocabulary.level: Background ng espesyal na termino · Advanced
vocabulary.cn: Medicaid (segurong pangkalusugan para sa mga may mababang kita na ibinibigay ng pederal at estado ng US)
vocabulary.en: a U.S. government program that provides health insurance to low-income individuals and families
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nag-aalala tungkol sa pagbawas ng Medicaid.

vocabulary.cn: Pagdami ng pagpapatupad ng batas; pagpapatindi ng mga aksyong pagpapatupad
vocabulary.en: a sudden increase in law enforcement activities, often targeting specific issues
vocabulary.excerpt: a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minne...
vocabulary.exampleCn: Pansamantalang itinigil ang pagdami ng pagpapatupad ng batas sa Maine at Minnesota.

vocabulary.en: a weapon that is thrown or fired through the air, especially one that is guided
vocabulary.excerpt: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Muling nagliwanag ang mga misayl sa kalangitan ng Gitnang Silangan.

vocabulary.en: a temporary agreement to stop fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ang tensyonadong tigil-putukan ay maaaring tumagal nang sapat na panahon?

vocabulary.level: Background ng Pangalan · Mataas na Antas
vocabulary.cn: Epstein Files (tumutukoy sa mga dokumento ng kaso na may kaugnayan sa yumaong sex offender na si Jeffrey Epstein)
vocabulary.en: documents related to the case of Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted sex offender
vocabulary.excerpt: a big day for the Epstein files.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Mahalagang araw ng Epstein Files.

vocabulary.en: a person who has been convicted of a sexual crime
vocabulary.excerpt: the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Yumaong nahatulang sex offender.

vocabulary.level: Teknikal na Terminolohiya · Mataas na antas
vocabulary.cn: panlalawigan; may kaugnayan sa halalan ng gobernador
vocabulary.en: relating to a governor or the election of a governor
vocabulary.excerpt: Colorado gubernatorial candidate.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kandidato para sa gobernador ng Colorado.

vocabulary.en: Initial Public Offering; the first sale of a company's shares to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Plano ng OpenAI at Anthropic na magsagawa ng Initial Public Offering.

vocabulary.cn: Ang bubble ay malapit nang pumutok.
vocabulary.en: a situation where an economic bubble is about to burst
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ang AI bubble ay malapit nang pumutok.

vocabulary.en: to indicate or show something, often indirectly
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Nagpahiwatig sa akin na ang AI bubble ay malapit nang pumutok.

vocabulary.en: a helicopter that has been shot down or forced to crash
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Matapos mabaril ang helicopter, sinalakay ng US ang Iran.

vocabulary.en: to attack or strike, especially militarily
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Matapos mabaril ang helicopter, sinalakay ng US ang Iran.

vocabulary.en: a person who makes laws; a member of a legislature
vocabulary.excerpt: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ano ang sasabihin ni Bill Gates sa mga mambabatas tungkol sa kanyang kaugnayan kay Epstein?

vocabulary.en: connections or relationships between people or things
vocabulary.excerpt: his links to the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他与已故的已定罪性犯罪者的联系。

vocabulary.en: to have committed murder when one was a child
vocabulary.excerpt: a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 一位还声称自己小时候杀过人的候选人。

sentences.en: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
sentences.cn: 缅因州的民主党人投票支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Democrats in Maine）+ 谓语（vote）+ 不定式短语（to stick with Graham Platner）作宾语。
sentences.analysis: stick with 意为"坚持支持"，是新闻常用短语。

sentences.en: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
sentences.cn: 这会让该党失去今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？
sentences.structure: Patanong: modal verb (Could) + subject (that) + predicate (cost) + indirect object (the party) + direct object (a shot at flipping the Senate this fall).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'cost sb. a shot at sth.' ay nangangahulugang 'mawalan ng pagkakataon ang isang tao na gawin ang isang bagay'; ang 'flip the Senate' ay tumutukoy sa 'pagbaligtad ng kontrol ng Senado', karaniwang ekspresyon sa balitang pampulitika.

sentences.en: If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
sentences.cn: Kung ang pagpipilian sa balota ay nasa pagitan ng iyon (tumutukoy sa kontrobersyal na aksyon ni Plattner) at isang senador na mag-aalis ng health insurance ng milyun-milyong Amerikano, iyon ang sitwasyong kailangan nating timbangin.
sentences.structure: Conditional adverbial clause (If...) + main clause (that's the situation) + relative clause (that we have to weigh).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'weigh' ay nangangahulugang 'timbangin', isang abstract verb; ang 'take health care away from' ay karaniwang paglalarawan ng negatibong patakaran sa balita.

sentences.en: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
sentences.cn: Magtatagal ba nang sapat ang tensyonadong ceasefire para matupad ng US ang matagal nang ipinangakong kasunduan sa Iran?
sentences.structure: Yes-no question: Will + subject (the strained ceasefire) + predicate (last) + adverbial (long enough) + infinitive phrase (for the U.S. to deliver...).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'strained ceasefire' ay nangangahulugang 'tensyonadong tigil-putukan'; ang 'long-promised' ay compound adjective na nangangahulugang 'matagal nang ipinangako'.

sentences.en: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
sentences.cn: Ano ang sasabihin ni Bill Gates sa mga mambabatas tungkol sa kanyang kaugnayan sa yumaong nahatulang sex offender?
sentences.structure: Wh-question: What + auxiliary (will) + subject (Bill Gates) + predicate (tell) + indirect object (lawmakers) + prepositional phrase (about his links...).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'the late convicted sex offender' ay tumutukoy sa 'yumaong nahatulang sex offender', ang 'late' ay nangangahulugang 'yumaong', ang 'convicted' ay past participle na ginamit bilang adjective.

sentences.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
sentences.cn: Sa tingin mo ba ay pumatay ka ng tao noong matanda ka na?
sentences.structure: Pangkalahatang tanong (Do you think) + sugnay na layon (that you've killed people as an adult).
sentences.analysis: Ang "as an adult" ay nangangahulugang "bilang isang may sapat na gulang", na ikinukumpara sa "as a child" sa susunod na bahagi.

sentences.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
sentences.cn: Plano ng OpenAI at Anthropic na magkaroon ng IPO sa katapusan ng taong ito, na nagpapahiwatig sa akin na malapit nang pumutok ang A.I. bubble.
sentences.structure: Pangunahing sugnay (OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs...) + pariralang pandiwang pangkasalukuyan (signaling to me that...) bilang pang-abay na resulta, na naglalaman ng sugnay na layon (that the A.I. bubble is about to pop).
sentences.analysis: Ang IPO ay daglat ng Initial Public Offering; ang "signal to sb. that..." ay nangangahulugang "magpahiwatig sa isang tao na..."; ang "be about to pop" ay nangangahulugang "malapit nang pumutok".

sentences.en: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support.
sentences.cn: Para sa sinumang nakakaramdam ng pagkabigo, pagkadismaya, o pagkawala ng ilusyon, ang aking trabaho ay makuha ang inyong tiwala, paniniwala, at suporta.
sentences.structure: Pariralang pang-ukol (To any of those...) bilang pang-abay, na naglalaman ng sugnay na panturing (who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned); pangunahing sugnay (it is my job to earn...).
sentences.analysis: Ang "let down" ay nangangahulugang "bigo"; ang "disillusioned" ay nangangahulugang "nawalan ng ilusyon"; ang "it is my job to do sth." ay isang karaniwang ekspresyon ng estrukturang pampadiin.

sentences.en: And I will spend every day of this campaign.
sentences.cn: Gugugulin ko ang bawat araw ng kampanyang ito.
sentences.structure: Payak na pangungusap: simuno (I) + panaguri (will spend) + layon (every day of this campaign).
sentences.analysis: Ang "spend" ay sinusundan ng pangngalang panahon, na nangangahulugang "gumugol ng oras".

sentences.en: And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
sentences.cn: Kung ako ay mapalad na gawin ang parehong bagay sa Senado ng Estados Unidos araw-araw.
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if I have the privilege...），其中包含现在分词短语（doing exactly that）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: have the privilege doing sth. 意为"有幸做某事"；the United States Senate 是专有名词。

sentences.en: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
sentences.cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Graham Platner）+ 谓语（punches）+ 宾语（his ticket）。
sentences.analysis: punch one's ticket 是习语，意为"获得（政治）提名或资格"。

sentences.en: But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator.
sentences.cn: 但现在他必须说服选民选择他而不是一位连任五届的参议员。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（he）+ 谓语（'s got to convince）+ 宾语（voters）+ 不定式短语（to choose him over...）。
sentences.analysis: convince sb. to do sth. 意为"说服某人做某事"；choose A over B 意为"选择A而不是B"；five-term 是复合形容词，意为"连任五届的"。

sentences.en: Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three-fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills.
sentences.cn: 民主党人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，尽管有所有丑闻，还是赢得了大约四分之三初选选民的支持，超过了州长珍妮特·米尔斯。
sentences.structure: 主语（Democrat Graham Platner）+ 插入语（despite all the scandals）+ 谓语（earned）+ 宾语（about three-fourths of primary voters' support）+ 状语（over Governor Janet Mills）。
sentences.analysis: despite 表示让步；three-fourths 是分数表达；over 在此表示"超过"。

sentences.en: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
sentences.cn: 在获得提名后不久，他就将注意力转向了他的新对手，共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。
sentences.structure: Panandang panahon (Moments after clinching the nomination) + pangunahing sugnay (he turned his attention to...), kung saan ang layon (his new opponent) ay sinusundan ng isang appositive (Republican Senator Susan Collins).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'clinch the nomination' ay nangangahulugang 'makamit ang nominasyon'; ang 'turn one's attention to' ay nangangahulugang 'ilipat ang atensyon sa'.

sentences.en: Susan Collins may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
sentences.cn: Maaaring sinimulan ni Susan Collins ang kanyang karera sa Washington ilang dekada na ang nakalipas na may mabubuting intensyon, ngunit siya ay naging kasing mahina at kasing corrupt ng establishment na kanyang pinaglilingkuran ngayon.
sentences.structure: Tambalang pangungusap: unang sugnay (Susan Collins may have started...) + but na nag-uugnay sa ikalawang sugnay (she has become...), na naglalaman ng paghahambing (as...as...) at isang relative clause (she now serves).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'may have started' ay nagpapahiwatig ng haka-haka tungkol sa nakaraan; ang 'spineless' ay nangangahulugang 'walang lakas ng loob'; ang 'the establishment' ay tumutukoy sa 'establishment'.

sentences.en: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
sentences.cn: Kung ikaw ay isang malayang boses, bakit 95% ng oras ay bumoboto ka kasama ni Donald Trump?
sentences.structure: Pangungusap na may kondisyonal na sugnay (If you are...) + pangunahing sugnay (why do you vote...).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'independent voice' ay nangangahulugang 'malayang boses'; ang 'vote with sb.' ay nangangahulugang 'bumoto kasama ang isang tao'.

sentences.en: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
sentences.cn: Maraming trabaho si Platner upang makuha ang suporta ng mga botante ni Mills.
sentences.structure: Payak na pangungusap: simuno (Platner) + panaguri (has) + layon (a lot of work) + infinitive phrase (to do to get...) bilang modifier at pang-abay na layunin.
sentences.analysis: Ang 'get sb. on one's side' ay nangangahulugang 'makuha ang suporta ng isang tao'.

sentences.en: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
sentences.cn: Hindi ko matapat na masuportahan si Platner, kahit na ang ilan sa kanyang mga sinabi ay talagang maganda.
sentences.structure: Pangunahing sugnay (I couldn't honestly support Platner) + sugnay na pananggalang (even though some of the things...), na may kasamang sugnay na naglalarawan (that he says).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'honestly' ay pang-abay na nagbibigay-turing sa 'support'; ang 'even though' ay nagpapakilala ng sugnay na pananggalang.

sentences.en: I don't think he's ready to be a senator.
sentences.cn: Sa palagay ko hindi pa siya handa maging senador.
sentences.structure: Pangunahing sugnay (I don't think) + sugnay na layon (he's ready to be a senator).
sentences.analysis: Paglipat ng negasyon: negatibo ang pangunahing sugnay, positibo ang sugnay na layon.

sentences.en: I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet.
sentences.cn: Mas pabor ako kay Collins, maliban kung may ipakita pa ang lalaki, pero hindi pa siya ganun.
sentences.structure: Tambalang pangungusap: unang sugnay (I'm leaning towards Collins unless...) + 'but' na nag-uugnay ng ikalawang sugnay (I just -- he's not there yet).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'lean towards' ay nangangahulugang 'pabor sa'; ang 'unless' ay nagpapakilala ng sugnay na kondisyonal; ang 'he's not there yet' ay kolokyal na ekspresyon na nangangahulugang 'hindi pa siya umaabot sa pamantayan'.

sentences.en: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
sentences.cn: Kung gayon, kung si Platner laban kay Collins sa Nobyembre, ano ang gagawin mo?
sentences.structure: Patanong na espesyal (what will you do...) + sugnay na kondisyonal (if it's Platner versus Collins).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'its' ay dapat na 'it's' (maling baybay); ang 'versus' ay nangangahulugang 'laban sa'.

sentences.en: I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine.
sentences.cn: Ako ay isang Democrat, ngunit maraming beses akong bumoto para kay Susan Collins dahil sa tingin ko talaga siyang nagmamalasakit sa Maine.
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一个分句（I'm a Democrat）+ but 连接第二个分句（I voted for Susan Collins several times），后跟原因状语从句（because I think...），其中包含宾语从句（that she does care...）。
sentences.analysis: does 表示强调；care about 意为"关心"。

sentences.en: But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
sentences.cn: 但这在十一月对我来说将是一个真正的问题。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（it）+ 谓语（'s going to be）+ 表语（a real problem）+ 状语（for me in November）。
sentences.analysis: be going to 表示将来；a real problem 意为"一个真正的问题"。

sentences.en: And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
sentences.cn: 马克斯，我实际上要从你开始，因为你在 VoteVets，这意味着你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。
sentences.structure: 主句（I'm actually going to start with you）+ 原因状语从句（because you're with VoteVets），后跟非限制性定语从句（which means...），其中包含宾语从句（you guys had a dog in the fight）。
sentences.analysis: have a dog in the fight 是习语，意为"有利害关系"。

sentences.en: Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
sentences.cn: 民主党翻转缅因州参议院席位的机会。
sentences.structure: 名词短语：主语（Democrats' chances）+ 介词短语（of flipping the Maine Senate seat）作定语。
sentences.analysis: flip the seat 意为"翻转席位（改变政党控制）"。

sentences.en: Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals?
sentences.cn: 既然格雷厄姆因这些丑闻而挣扎，这场竞选是否像你们所有人希望的那样接近？
sentences.structure: Pangkalahatang tanong (Is this race as close as...) + Paghahambing na estruktura (as...as...) + Sugnay na layon (you all hoped it would be) + Sugnay na dahilan (now that...).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'as close as' ay nangangahulugang 'kasing lapit ng'; ang 'now that' ay nangangahulugang 'dahil'.

sentences.en: The race is always going to be close.
sentences.cn: Ang karerang ito ay palaging magiging malapit.
sentences.structure: Payak na pangungusap: Paksa (The race) + Panaguri (is always going to be) + Panaguring pang-uri (close).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'be going to' ay nagpapahiwatig ng hinaharap; ang 'close' dito ay nangangahulugang 'magkapatas'.

sentences.en: Not with a five-term senator, it's not.
sentences.cn: Hindi ito malapit sa isang senador na limang termino na, hindi.
sentences.structure: Pinaikling pangungusap: Ang buong anyo ay 'It is not close with a five-term senator.'
sentences.analysis: Ang 'five-term' ay isang tambalang pang-uri; ang 'it's not' ay pinaikling ekspresyon.

sentences.en: What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race.
sentences.cn: Tungkol sa kampanya ni Susan Collins, ang sasabihin ko ay, ang nakita natin sa nakaraan ay, hindi mo makukuha ang puwestong ito sa pamamagitan ng paghalal ng 'Susan Collins light' o paglalabas ng 'Susan Collins light' sa kampanya.
sentences.structure: Sugnay na paksa (What I would say...) + Pandiwang nag-uugnay (is) + Sugnay na panaguri (that we've seen...), kung saan ang sugnay na panaguri ay naglalaman ng isa pang sugnay na panaguri (is you're not going to win...).
sentences.analysis: Ang 'Susan Collins light' ay nangangahulugang 'mahinang bersyon ni Susan Collins', ang 'light' ay nangangahulugang 'magaan'; ang 'put forward' ay nangangahulugang 'ilabas'.

sentences.en: You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
sentences.cn: Matatalo mo siya sa pamamagitan ng pagpapasigla sa iyong pangunahing botante, lalo na sa midterm elections.
sentences.structure: Payak na pangungusap: Paksa (You) + Panaguri (are going to beat) + Layon (her) + Pang-abay na paraan (by energizing your base) + Pahayag na pampasok (particularly in a midterm election).
sentences.analysis: Ang "energize your base" ay nangangahulugang "pasiglahin ang iyong pangunahing suporta"; ang "midterm election" ay tumutukoy sa "halalan sa gitna ng termino".

topics.title: Halalan sa Senado ng Maine: Platner vs. Collins
topics.content: Sa Democratic primary ng Maine, tinalo ng kontrobersyal na si Graham Platner si Gobernador Janet Mills na may humigit-kumulang 75% ng boto, at haharapin ang limang beses na Republican Senator na si Susan Collins. Kailangan ni Platner na makuha ang suporta ng mga tagasuporta ni Mills, samantalang si Collins ay may kalamangan pa rin sa mga independent voter. Nais ng Democrats na makuha ang puwesto, ngunit ang isyu sa karakter ni Platner ay maaaring makaapekto sa resulta.
topics.keywords: Graham Platner · Susan Collins · primary · flip · independent voter

topics.title: Pag-atake ng US sa Iran
topics.content: Nagpaputok ng missile ang US sa Iran matapos mabaril ang isang helicopter, na nagpapataas ng tensyon sa Middle East. Dati ay may kasunduan ang US at Iran, ngunit ang ceasefire ay nasa ilalim ng presyon. Ang insidente ay maaaring makaapekto sa katatagan ng rehiyon at patakarang panlabas ng US.
topics.keywords: missile · Iran · ceasefire · U.S. military strike · helicopter downed

topics.title: IPO ng AI Companies at Pangamba sa Bubble
topics.content: Plano ng OpenAI at Anthropic na magsagawa ng Initial Public Offering (IPO) bago matapos ang taon, na nagdudulot ng pangamba na sasabog ang A.I. bubble. Nag-aalala ang mga mamumuhunan kung sobra ang halaga ng AI industry at kung ang IPO ay magiging tanda ng pagbabago sa sektor.
topics.keywords: IPO · OpenAI · Anthropic · A.I. bubble · pop

topics.title: Kontrobersyal na Pahayag ng Kandidato sa Gobernador ng Colorado
topics.content: Ang Republican candidate para sa gobernador ng Colorado na si Victor Marx ay nagsabing pumatay siya ng tao noong bata pa siya, at nagtanong, "Mahalaga ba iyon?", na nagdulot ng kontrobersya. Siya ang frontrunner sa Republican primary, at ang kanyang matinding pahayag ay maaaring makaapekto sa resulta ng halalan.
topics.keywords: Victor Marx · gubernatorial · frontrunner · controversial · killed someone

quiz.question: Sa 'Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner', ang pinakamalapit na kahulugan ng 'stick with' ay?
quiz.explanation: Ang 'stick with' ay isang phrasal verb na nangangahulugang 'patuloy na suportahan o piliin'. Dito, nangangahulugan ito na nagpasya ang mga Democrat na patuloy na suportahan si Platner. Ang ibang pagpipilian ay literal o kabaligtaran.

quiz.question: Sa 'the A.I. bubble is about to pop', ang kahulugan ng 'pop' ay?
quiz.explanation: Ang 'pop' dito ay nangangahulugang 'pumutok', tumutukoy sa pagputok ng bubble. Sa konteksto ng ekonomiya, madalas gamitin ang 'bubble pops' para sa pagbagsak ng bubble.

quiz.question: Sa 'Graham Platner punches his ticket', ang ibig sabihin ng 'punches his ticket' ay?
quiz.explanation: Ang 'punch one's ticket' ay isang idyoma na nangangahulugang 'makakuha ng nominasyon o kwalipikasyon', lalo na sa konteksto ng politika na nangangahulugang matagumpay na umusad.

quiz.question: Ayon sa teksto, ano ang pangunahing hamon na kinaharap ni Platner matapos manalo sa primarya?
options: A. Mangalap ng pondo para sa kampanya
B. Makuha ang suporta ng mga botante ni Mills
C. Pagharap sa mga paratang ng iskandalo
D. Pagkuha ng suporta mula sa partido
quiz.explanation: Binanggit sa teksto na kailangan ni Platner na 'get Mills voters on his side', ibig sabihin, makuha ang suporta ng mga botante ni Mills, dahil natalo si Mills sa primarya at ang kanyang mga botante ay maaaring lumipat kay Collins.

quiz.question: Alin sa mga sumusunod ang tamang paglalarawan tungkol kay Susan Collins?
options: B. Siya ay isang limang terminong senador
D. Siya ay mula sa Demokratikong partido
quiz.explanation: Tinatawag ng teksto si Collins na "five-term senator", ibig sabihin ay limang termino na senador. Hindi siya malapit kay Trump ('not a tight bond'), at siya ay isang Republikano.

quiz.question: Tungkol sa AI IPO, ano ang pananaw na binanggit sa teksto?
options: A. Malapit nang pumutok ang AI bubble
B. Makatuwiran ang pagpapahalaga ng mga AI company
C. Ang IPO ay magtutulak sa pag-unlad ng AI
D. Ang merkado ay may tiwala sa AI
quiz.explanation: Ang pananaw na binanggit sa teksto ay "the A.I. bubble is about to pop", ibig sabihin ay malapit nang pumutok ang AI bubble, na nagpapahiwatig na ang IPO ay maaaring senyales ng rurok ng bubble.
